venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Country | Folk | Americana If nerditude had a formal name, it would be Grace Morrison.   She's a little bit of everything-pianist, accordion player, Renaissance Faire performer, cranberry grower, reader of historical nonfiction, coffee devotee (she sells her own coffee blend), and an unapologetic expert on all things New England. And somehow, all these quirks weave seamlessly into the fabric of her music, making her one of the most unique and endearing songwriters around. Born and raised on the shores of Cape Cod, Grace Morrison has trademarked a sound she calls Saltwater Country. "I was always too pop for folk and too folk for country. Eventually, I started peeling back the layers of my music to find out what truly made it mine. At the heart of it all was my deep, undeniable connection to the Cape Cod coastline-it's in my blood, in my voice, in every lyric I write. My music carries the storytelling of country, the twang, but also the raw, unshakable spirit of a Swamp Yankee. That's Saltwater Country."

Country With a presence as powerful as her story, Lilly Moss channels resilience and rare authenticity into everything she does-on stage and off. At just 19, she released her debut album Meet Me on Broadway (2024), earning multiple placements on Spotify's All New Country playlist and quickly emerging as an artist to watch. With new music arriving in 2026, Lilly will debut unreleased tracks including "Diggin' in My Heels," "Death of Me," and "Somebody Should," giving audiences an exclusive first listen ahead of their official release. With soaring vocals and raw emotional honesty, she blends heartfelt storytelling with a modern country edge, solidifying her status as one of country music's most compelling new voices.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Folk | Americana | Oldies JOE LAZORIK is a Lehigh Valley piano player specializing in event music, formal shows, party entertainment and sing-alongs. With over five decades of professional piano playing experience, Joe allows his versatile style to adapt to various needs as in addition to providing background music, he is very capable of interacting with and entertaining an audience. His song inventory samples turn of the century classics, American standards, folk songs, ballads, patriotic selections, show tunes, TV theme songs, and hits of the 1950's, 60's & 70's!   In addition to performing again at Musikfest, Joe has provided music for several ArtsQuest events over the years at the Musikfest Café, Christkindlmarkt, Palette & Pour and former Banana Factory, plus local venues including the ABE Airport, DeSales University, and various Country Clubs in our area. He also entertains weekly at many senior facilities in the region performing his popular "Great American Piano Show" for their residents & guests.

Alternative | Party/Cover | Rock Groove Therapy is a high-energy 7-piece band made up of talented musicians from some of the Lehigh Valley's top bands. Featuring two powerhouse female lead vocalists, dual lead guitars, keys, bass, and drums. We deliver a dynamic, full-band sound that gets people on their feet.   Our music spans from classic rock to current pop, blending rock, alternative, dance hits, and today's country into a fun, modern mix. Every song is chosen to be familiar, energetic, and danceable-keeping the crowd moving and the party going all night long.   Groove Therapy brings the perfect combination of musicianship, energy, and crowd-pleasing favorites to every performance.

Alternative | Rock | Scuba Rock Fez is a central Pennsylvania scuba‑rock trio from State College, Pennsylvania, formed in 2022 by vocalist and drummer Xavier Hons and bassist Max Hill, and completed shortly after by guitarist Jimmy Connelly. The band quickly developed a reputation for high‑energy shows, sharp musicianship, and playful crowdwork across the East Coast. On stage, Fez brings a tight, high‑voltage set that has included campus marquee moments such as the Movin' On festival, and a full live studio performance for 90.7 FM that captures the band's chemistry in a broadcast setting. Press coverage has underscored the group's individuality and home‑grown momentum, including the feature "State College's Fez 'sounds like itself'," highlighted on the band's website. With an ever‑expanding fan base, a growing discography, and a live show that feels immediate, inventive, and unmistakably Fez, the band continues to write, record, and perform with the intensity of a group that has found its voice and its audience.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Alternative | Pop | Rock Young and sweet, only seventeen...Lorelei and Francis are a piano and guitar duo who love to sing together. They're both seasoned performers, ready to switch things up and keep the set moving between laid-back moments and more energetic ones. Their sound pulls from rock, pop, and alternative genres, mixing familiar songs with a fresh feel and a couple originals along the way.

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Alternative | Rock | Garage The Dracu-Las are a vocal harmony-driven Rock 'n' Roll outfit based in Jersey City. Formed in the fall of 2021, they play garage Rock 'N Roll with elements of punk, power pop, surf, psych, post punk, and 60's girl groups sprinkled throughout. The band is comprised of Kyna Damewood (lead vocals, guitar), Susan Lutin (bass, backing vocals), Dave Dufrane (guitar), and Mitch Cady (drums).    The four-piece turned heads with their debut EP, Fever Dream, released in December 2022 on cassette via State Champion Records. They followed up with their first 7" titled Fall Asleep When I'm Dead, released in 2024 on Power Trash Records. A second 7", I'm Blue, is out now on Outro Records.

Funk | Jam Band It is often said that the private eye works in the shadows, quietly gathering information In the pursuit of evidence. Silence follows the spy. For there must be no trace. Suspense, Intrigue, Excitement. Every motion must be carefully calculated, if the spy is to remain undetected.   Special Characters embraces the ebb and flow of the eternal mission, maintaining funky excellence and liquid grooves. Personnel includes Sammy Ciravolo (guitar), Mike Allen (drums), Tristan Haraschak (keyboards, flugelhorn), and Justin Sollene (bass). Specialist's in their own respective territory, this band of vagabonds has what it takes to make you want to shake. (Your booty that is)

artist: Poets for Justice Presents..."Rooted & Rising" Spoken Word Showcase
description: Sunday, August 2 at 3 p.m. and 6 p.m. The Ice House    Step into a space designed by the words of wisdom to reflect the unapologetic beauty of being a person of color.    Poets for Justice is a Lehigh Valley based platform founded with the intention to create a safe space for local artists to speak to injustice.

artist: Lehigh Valley Charter High School for the Arts Contemporary Music Ensemble
venue: Lutron Jazz Cabaret Stage at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Alternative | Party/Cover | Rock The Charter Arts Contemporary Music Ensemble is an ensemble that performs music from rock/pop mediums from all eras of the 20th and 21st centuries! The ensemble is made up of both vocal and instrumental students from 11th and 12th grade at the Lehigh Valley Charter High School for the Arts.

Funk R&B | Soul | Classic Rock We are a high-energy, nine-piece band that brings you the best of funk, soul, R&B, and smooth jazz, with an impressive resume that includes over 20 years at the Berks Jazz Fest, this will be our 4th year at Musikfest, and a 2020 induction into the Berks County Rock and Roll Hall of Fame.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Alternative | Indie | Singer/Songwriter Maddy Kas is a Lehigh Valley-based singer, guitarist, and songwriter, and the 2025 winner of the Lehigh Valley Sing-Off, a local singer-songwriter competition. Her original music draws from personal experience and introspection, focusing on thoughtful lyrics and emotional connection. Her music is available on all major streaming platforms. Alongside her original work, Maddy performs throughout the Lehigh Valley and surrounding areas, primarily playing classic rock and contemporary covers. She reshapes familiar songs through her distinctive vocal style, guitar approach, and emotional delivery. Whether sharing her own stories or reimagining well-known favorites, she brings an upbeat, sincere, and engaging presence to every performance.

Latin | Pop Talento Local is a vibrant group of seniors from the Hispanic Center proudly showcasing their musical and dance talents throughout the Lehigh Valley. Through folkloric and Spanish music and dance performances, they bring joy, culture, laughter, and entertainment to audiences of all ages.

Indie | Pop | Rock Cathy Ritter is the emerging singer-songwriter the pop industry has waited for. Raised by two musicians in southeast Pennsylvania, Cathy has a deeply rooted connection to poetic story-telling through songs that she has showcased in Allentown, Philadelphia, and throughout New York City. Cathy writes of love in all its forms, and tells her own coming-of-age story earnestly through an energetic acoustic vessel in original songs like "Witching Hour", "Green" and "Under the Radar". Her voice is unmistakably authentic, as it blends her warm tone with both distinct power and an intentional softness, as heard in her songs "Alpenglow" and "Blanket". She strives to break ground as a versatile and unique vocalist and songwriter while paying homage to the great female singers that she has trained to emulate since girlhood, not limited to the timeless voices of Ann Wilson and Bonnie Raitt, as well as today's big-voice pop icons such as Lady Gaga, Adele, and Kelly Clarkson.

Alternative | Folk | Americana | Indie My Familiar blends raw emotion with soulful indie rock, threading folk, grit, and guts into every song. Based in Philly, we write music that hits where it hurts and heals. Harmonies, violins, and big feelings-come feel something.

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Blues | Indie | Rock It all began six years ago when Jack, Peyton, Reese & Miles met at music school. And three years ago decided to form a band that fused their creativity with classic rock, 90s grunge and a little bit of blues and funk. After three-plus years performing more than 100+ shows from New York to Nashville, Sunrise Cries is performing their soulful originals with a sound that could be called new classic rock. Find their new original album, Solar Opposition, on all streaming services.

Country | Pop | Rock Singer songwriter Stefanie Johnson is native to the Lehigh Valley. Her style ranges from pop artist to rock singer with a slight country timbre as well. Similarly, her music and songwriting approach traverse those same motifs. In addition to the local scene, Stefanie has performed at Nashville's legendary Bluebird Cafe and 12th & Porter, as well as the Rock and Roll of Fame in Cleveland, Ohio. Her songs have been featured in several films most recently including Daniel Roebuck's Saint Nick of Bethlehem and The Hail Mary. She has been performing with her all-star band for many years, featuring her co-writer, multi-instrumentalist/studio owner Brian Anthony, super talented guitarist Michael Anthus and dynamic veteran drummer Tom Chaffier to deliver a mix of country, rock, and pop that is sure to provide something for everyone.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Alternative | Party/Cover | Rock Brian has loved string instruments since he was a small child, making his first guitar at age seven with a shoe box and rubber bands over an open shoe. He began studying cello at age nine, and learned guitar at age fourteen. By 9th grade, Brian fell in love with rock and roll. When his parents grounded him, due to chronic after-school detention, he was left without his newfound, beloved guitar. With optimistic defiance, he taught himself how to play the electric guitar parts on his cello. Brian makes his own arrangements to cover by popular artists, including Billy Joel, Frank Sinatra, System of a Down, Blink-182, Linkin Park and many more.  Brian also enjoys home music multi-track recording, and his latest single is called "Inside." He is working on several other original songs, including the bluesy "Love You Better," which has an impressive cello solo, a pop song called "Pretty Girl" and a ballad called "Twilight Sea."

Alternative | Indie | Pop | Rap/Hip-Hop Faith Sedicum is a 16 year old singer-songwriter from Bucks County, PA. This year she is returning to Musikfest with originals from her debut album, "Hallowween in February" and from the newly released album "Taking My Flowers Back".

venue: Lutron Jazz Cabaret Stage at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Classical Keystone Brass represents the professionalism and versatility of Army National Guard musicians. Performing classical, ceremonial, patriotic, and contemporary works, the ensemble provides musical support for official functions, community events, and public concerts while serving as ambassadors of the Pennsylvania Army National Guard.

Blues | Classical | County | Folk Affiliation with the state PMTA and National MTNA professional organizations enable teachers to take advantage of member collaboration and networking; student performances in recitals, auditions, and master classes, continuing education for membership era at workshops, master classes, state/national conferences, student field trips, summer camp scholarship assistance, and scholarship competitions for senior high school students continuing music study at college. Social Media Information Artist website: lvmusicteachers.org

Blues Steel Town Blues is a Bethlehem, Pennsylvania-based blues band that channels the spirit of the Steel City with a high‑energy mix of classic and contemporary blues‑rock. Their sound is shaped by decades of listening to and playing favorites from Stevie Ray Vaughan, Eric Clapton, Albert Collins, Muddy Waters, Freddie King, Little Walter, and other blues artists. Front man Rick Young (vocals, harmonica) brings decades of experience from Reading‑area blues bands, while keyboardist Robert Victor who is also a talented arranger and producer adds boogie piano and soulful organ. Lead guitarist Robert Cassidy and drummer George Karas, veterans of Bethlehem's Bedlam Blues Band, lock in with bassist Geoff Fleming, whose time with the Chuck Lambert Blues Band fuels the group's deep, swinging groove. With roots up and down the Lehigh Valley, the Jersey Shore, and the Mid‑Atlantic club circuit, Steel Town Blues delivers crowd‑pleasing sets that keep audiences on their feet.

venue: Wind Creek Steel Stage at PNC Plaza
description: With special guest St. Lucia Since 1997, San Francisco's Third Eye Blind have recorded ten best-selling studio recordings, two live releases and assembled one career retrospective selling 12 million records worldwide. Led by Stephan Jenkins, 3EB won wide success during a tumultuous group of years when the major-label recording industry was finally losing its grip on an enterprise that for decades it had dominated with steely efficiency. Nothing could have made 3EB happier! 3EB, however, has experienced no comparable loss. Instead, they have gained artistic clarification — and, surprisingly, a fan base who is larger, younger and more dedicated than ever. Participation in the older, untouchable realm of nervous star-making could color a band's identity. In the case of 3EB, it often blurred the perception of their brilliant musical creations. In recent years, those creations have recast the band among a current generation of fans. 3EB now write, tour, record, and communicate in a fluid new world where their music continues to evolve naturally. Their exchange with their audience is unfiltered and being from the hub of tech, they are using it to develop a closer connection with their audience. Special Guest: St. Lucia Led by husband-and-wife duo South Africa-born Jean-Philip Grobler and Germany-born Patti Beranek, St. Lucia retreat from a fractured world on fire and into the glow of a rapturous synth-spiked electro-pop catharsis—like a dance party at the edge of existence. Now signed to Nettwerk Records, the group beams out a light of their own and shines brighter than ever. Partners

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Funk | Latin | R&B | Soul | Rock The only fusion band in PA

Jazz | Ragtime | New Orleans Jazz Blair Crimmins began his current music career in Atlanta, Georgia, with a drive to bring Ragtime and 1920's style Dixieland Jazz to new audiences. While playing small rock clubs around the Southeast he developed a sound that is at once modern while being deeply rooted in the past. He has toured the U.S and Italy playing festivals and has opened for acts such as Preservation Hall Jazz Band and Old Crow Medicine show. A multiinstrumentalist on guitar, banjo and piano, Crimmins writes songs and arrangements for a classic New Orleans style horn section consisting of trumpet, clarinet and trombone. His debut release "The Musical Stylings Of" became a college radio sensation on WRAS Atlanta making him the most requested band on the air. In 2012 Crimmins showed his musical diversity by writing and recording the full score for the independent short film "Old Man Cabbage". The following year, Crimmins was the critics pick for Best Song Writer of 2013 in Creative Loafing's Best of ATL issue.

artist: Poets for Justice Presents..."Rooted & Rising" Spoken Word Showcase
description: Sunday, August 2 at 3 p.m. and 6 p.m. The Ice House    Step into a space designed by the words of wisdom to reflect the unapologetic beauty of being a person of color.    Poets for Justice is a Lehigh Valley based platform founded with the intention to create a safe space for local artists to speak to injustice.

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Country | Folk | Americana | Rock Born and raised in New Jersey, singer-songwriter Sean Marshall has since resided at the Jersey Shore, the Midwest, the Mountain West, and now the Lehigh Valley Pennsylvania. His experience across diverse landscapes and audiences seeps through his songs with an eclectic vision of America and its people. 4 full-length studio albums display this sonic range, with sounds rooted in classic folk structures, enriched with blues textures, and laced with country ambiance.

Country A Whiskey and Roses show is not a passive viewing experience; it is an event. Each member was selected not just for their ability to play, but for their ability to entertain. They bring an insurmountable amount of talent and energy to the stage, creating an interactive, high-energy atmosphere that demands crowd participation.    They are the ultimate country party band, armed with a repertoire that spans decades of country hits with a shot of rock. From the classic anthems that everyone knows the words to, to the latest chart-topping modern country bangers, W&R knows exactly how to read a room and keep the dance floor packed all night long.

Children's/Family | Street Performer | Percussion Orbital is a percussion-centric music entertainment group located in Bethlehem, PA. The group features various drumline style routines, choreographed percussion shows and even bagpipes. The group believes in empowering musicians and creating jaw-dropping live musical performances.      Orbital Drumline was founded in April of 2018 by a group of five friends. With a set of used drums we found on Facebook Marketplace and a dream, we set out to create an entertainment percussive experience like no other. Hundreds of shows later - we are still going strong!      The group is made up entirely of Liberty High School alumni and former Grenadier Band Members and almost all of their head drummers from 2017-2025. We are proud of our Bethlehem roots and have received training and mentorship from world renowned musicians and percussionists like Stu Warmington, Tyler Fry, and members of the Red Hot Chili Pipers.

Indie | Pop | R&B | Soul Audrey Kreitner is a pop r&b singer-songwriter from Hunterdon County, NJ. Through her work blending genres, she created a captivating sound of her own. Audrey has graced the stages of Musikfest, Bitter End, Stone Pony, and plenty more. Audrey is in the final stages of completing her EP and will release her first album shortly after. She has music available on all streaming platforms and has collaborated with artists across a range of styles. On top of Audrey's artistic work, she is currently in her second year at Berklee College of Music, honing her craft and networking her way to the top.

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Alternative | Indie | Rock Raw, melodic, and loud, Ruby Dear is a powerhouse rock trio from Bethlehem, PA, delivering a high-voltage mix of rock, alt, and post-punk. Featuring Jenny Founds (vocals/guitar), Jon Braun (vocals/drums), and Jesse Braun (vocals/bass), the band formed after years touring together in the Talking Heads tribute Start Making Sense. Founds honed her craft performing with icons like Jon Anderson (Yes), Gibby Haynes (Butthole Surfers), and Perry Farrell (Jane's Addiction), before teaming up with the Braun brothers, veterans of The Insidious Rays and Great White Caps. With two albums (Ruby Dear, This Will) and a reputation for fierce, tightly-honed live shows, Ruby Dear delivers original songs that hit hard and leave a mark.

Indie Jim Rezac is an Award Winning songwriter from Delaware. This will be his 7th year performing MUSIKFEST!   His songs have been described as 'honest'...and he's been compared to W. Nelson, J. Hiatt, J. Isbell with a touch of Orbison sometimes. He has played places like the Bitter End in NYC, the Avalon in Easton, MD, and Freeman Arts Pavilion and just about every songwriter showcase he can find!   This year he brings his bassist (and brother, Joe) to join him for the first time. Together, they are know as the ChurchKey Saints.   When they're not playing the beaches of Delaware you can find Jim Rezac in his humble studio by the tracks in Wonderful Wyoming Delaware. Jim took home 'Best Theme Song for a Podcast' at the NJ Web Fest (international awards event) and just last July... while he was preparing for another Musikfest... the Delaware native got ot hear his song, 'Tryin' 2 Be An Angel' as part of the soundtrack for the feature film, Meathook (Cutting Floor Films, L.A.)

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Country | Folk | Americana If nerditude had a formal name, it would be Grace Morrison.   She's a little bit of everything-pianist, accordion player, Renaissance Faire performer, cranberry grower, reader of historical nonfiction, coffee devotee (she sells her own coffee blend), and an unapologetic expert on all things New England. And somehow, all these quirks weave seamlessly into the fabric of her music, making her one of the most unique and endearing songwriters around. Born and raised on the shores of Cape Cod, Grace Morrison has trademarked a sound she calls Saltwater Country. "I was always too pop for folk and too folk for country. Eventually, I started peeling back the layers of my music to find out what truly made it mine. At the heart of it all was my deep, undeniable connection to the Cape Cod coastline-it's in my blood, in my voice, in every lyric I write. My music carries the storytelling of country, the twang, but also the raw, unshakable spirit of a Swamp Yankee. That's Saltwater Country."

Jazz | Latin | World Cocomama is a New York-based multinational, all-female ensemble blending Latin jazz, Afro-Cuban rhythms, salsa, soul, and R&B into a dynamic global sound. Formed in 2005, the group has built a two-decade career rooted in collaborative creation and cross-cultural exchange, performing at venues including the United Nations, the Brooklyn Academy of Music, and the Metropolitan Museum of Art. Their debut album Quiero earned 2016 Grammy pre-nomination recognition, while the self-produced Woman's World (2021) highlighted the ensemble's commitment to collective voice and artistic independence. Known for performances that move seamlessly between rhythmic intensity and lyrical depth, Cocomama continues to expand the language of Latin jazz through genre-fluid composition and diverse musical influences. Their newest project, Evolución (2025), supported by Chamber Music America grants, celebrates the group's twentieth anniversary with collaborative songwriting and a renewed focus on innovation.

Country | Folk | Americana | Rock Reservoir Hill is an original country band from Allentown, PA, formed in 2018 by singer-songwriters Dylan Flaherty and Brad Kramer. Blending modern country with Southern rock, folk, and jam-band influences, the band is known for honest songwriting and high-energy, crowd-engaging performances.   Their self-titled debut EP (2019) earned a Lehigh Valley Music Awards nomination for the single "Boneyard," followed by the full-length albums Desperate Times (2021) and Leave It All Behind (2023), highlighted by a headlining performance at the Levitt Pavilion in Bethlehem, PA.   The current lineup features Dylan Flaherty (vocals/guitar), Brad Kramer (vocals/guitar), Tommy Pogash (bass), Brian J. Anthony (bass), Abigail Zuckert (piano/vocals), Nygel Flaherty (rhythm guitar/mandolin/vocals), and Shohn Montano (drums). With four lead vocalists and a dynamic live show, Reservoir Hill continues to captivate audiences across the Northeast and beyond.

Country | Folk | Americana | Rock John Hollier has been experiencing a handful of full-circle moments lately. Originally from Louisiana, the rising singer-songwriter has been relentlessly chasing down not only his unique sound - this searing fusion of rock, soul and alt-country - but also himself in the process. The result is his latest album, Rainmaker, a record showcasing Hollier's live wire talent and artistic purpose.   "The live show is where I feel we've been able to really latch on to an identity. I don't feel like the previous record captured that identity," Hollier says candidly. "In terms of standing out, I think the show quickly outpaced that album. This new record is a true representation of the past two years."

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Swamp Soul Marcella Simien is a Louisiana-born artist and a proud ninth-generation Creole. Her music is a soulful reflection of her deeply personal journey through spirituality and identity. Daughter of two-time GRAMMY winning Zydeco icon Terrance Simien, Marcella has been developing her music career for 20 years. With a genre-defying sound that blends Soul, American Roots, Blues, Jazz, and more, Simien crafts a sonic experience that is both timeless and uniquely her own. Drawing from the rich cultural heritage of her lineage, she bridges tradition and innovation with powerful authenticity and heart. She brings this same care to her work in the yoga and wellness sphere. When she's not on the road she is involved in community work in Memphis. Simien is a registered yoga and meditation instructor, an Orpheum Theatre Group Teaching Artist and Teaching Artist with the Wolf Trap Institute for Early Learning Through the Arts.

venue: Lutron Jazz Cabaret Stage at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Blues | Jazz | Modern Jazz Saxophonist, Dan Wilkins returns on a decade plus of performances at Musikfest with his quartet, playing music from recent releases, plus new material. Wilkins released 'Moonglow' on Deer Head Records in 2024 featuring his own reimagining of well known music from jazz and Great American Songbook repertoire, and 'Oceans Beyond the Sky' on the Each and Only record label, featuring Wilkins' original music inspired by dreams. Wilkins quartet performs regionally at Smalls Jazz Club in NYC, Allentown's Miller Symphony hall, Deer Head Inn, Vic's Jazz Loft, Chris' Jazz Cafe, Ice House, COTA Jazz Festival, Wilkes Barre Cavalcade of Jazz and Kennett Jazz Festival among others. The quartet looks forward to the immersive theater experience, performing inside the Frank Banko Alehouse Cinema. Come enjoy the music.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Party/Cover | Pop | R&B | Soul Jake Ortiz is a bilingual pop artist delivering a bold blend of late night energy, raw emotion, and undeniable stage presence. Jake crafts music that lives in the space between seductive and vulnerable, where desire, control, and connection collide.   Based in New Jersey, Jake has quickly built a reputation as a dynamic live performer. His recent milestones include a televised performance appearance and performing at notable venues and events across the region, including popular casinos Golden Nugget, Hard Rock, and Borgata. As well as many prides across the tri-state and many restaurants, Jake continues to expand his reach and audience with every show.   Jake's performances are more than just sets. They're experiences. With a signature style that fuses modern pop with an edgy, magnetic persona, he pulls audiences into his world from the first note to the last. Jake Ortiz is an artist on the rise, one who isn't just looking to perform, but to leave a lasting impression.

Alternative | Indie | Rock Joy and the Wildfire is a high-voltage indie rock band with big hooks and a magnetic edge. Led by powerhouse vocalist Samantha Joy Pearlman, the NYC-based band blends gritty storytelling with arena-ready anthems, drawing comparisons to icons like Debbie Harry and Paramore. Come for the music. Stay for the curls.   "...a band to watch out for [and] inevitably fall in love with" - CLOUT Magazine  "A Seismic Shift in the Indie Rock landscape" -MusicEarShot.Com  "A voice like if silk and fire had a baby"- Illustrate Magazine  "Electrifying Storytelling and a Fearless Approach to Rock"- RotateMag

Country | Party/Cover | Rock Cody Templeton & Co. deliver modern country with a rock edge and the kind of high-impact energy today's venues demand. Fronted by Lehigh Valley, PA native Cody Templeton, a powerhouse vocalist with undeniable authenticity, the band brings a polished, crowd-winning show built on singalong country hits and crossover rock favorites that keep dance floors packed. Cody's rise has already landed him on stages alongside national acts like Parmalee, Grammy nominated & ACM winner Jimmie Allen, and Colt Ford, proving he can hold his own with the best. Backed by a tight, no-frills band featuring Tommy Harris and Joe Schneider (guitars), Nick Herzing (bass), and Bennett Rose (drums), this group delivers a powerful, high-energy performance every time. Already turning heads at Wind Creek Casino's Molten Lounge and Atlantic City hotspots, Cody Templeton & Co. are quickly becoming a go-to for venues looking to elevate their lineup.

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Tribute Frontiers is the world's number one tribute to Journey. These five talented, skilled, renowned NYC area musicians recreate the music of Journey to perfection, note for note - every nuance, every iconic riff, all the feels. Frontiers covers all the greatest hits of Journey, taking you back to the late 70's during Journey's magical inception with Gregg Rolie and Neal Schon, and to the 80's with Steve Perry and Jonathan Cain, when Journey's timeless, chart topping hits such as Open Arms, Separate Ways, Send Her My Love, Faithfully, Stone in Love, Don't Stop Believing, Who's Crying Now, Anyway You Want It, and many others ruled the airwaves.

Blues | Rock | Tribute Members of the popular Craig Thatcher Band (Billy Wear, Chico Huff, Cliff Starkey, Joel Shimer & Craig) playing an exciting variety of blues, rock, folk and light jazz-original music & covers, acoustic and electric, done in their own inimitable style. Craig has been playing this intimate stage at Musikfest since 1989 and always enjoys coming back here where the band can be up close and personal with the audience.

Latin | Gospel Orquesta Xariz was founded in 1989 by Ruben Felix in New York City. Orquesta Xariz has been spreading the Gospel through the vibrant rhythm of salsa for more than 35 years. Beginning his musical journey at just nine years old, Ruben went on to perform with legendary artists such as Richie Ray and Bobby Cruz, establishing Xariz as a leading voice in Christian Latin music.   Driven by faith, Ruben has written more than 100 original songs, with over 50 recorded songs. Orquesta Xariz has released eight productions and has inspired audiences across the United States, Latin America, and the Caribbean.   In 2024, Ruben was honored with the Tropical Song of the Year award by Premios Radikal. The group has also been recognized by New York City for its dedicated outreach in correctional facilities, including Riker's Island.   Now based in Allentown, Pennsylvania, Orquesta Xariz continues its mission of sharing faith, hope, and joy with the world through the power and passion of salsa.

Indie Jim Rezac is an Award Winning songwriter from Delaware. This will be his 7th year performing MUSIKFEST!   His songs have been described as 'honest'...and he's been compared to W. Nelson, J. Hiatt, J. Isbell with a touch of Orbison sometimes. He has played places like the Bitter End in NYC, the Avalon in Easton, MD, and Freeman Arts Pavilion and just about every songwriter showcase he can find!   This year he brings his bassist (and brother, Joe) to join him for the first time. Together, they are know as the ChurchKey Saints. Jim took home 'Best Theme Song for a Podcast' at the NJ Web Fest (international awards event) and just last July... while he was preparing for another Musikfest... the Delaware native got ot hear his song, 'Tryin' 2 Be An Angel' as part of the soundtrack for the feature film, Meathook (Cutting Floor Films, L.A.)

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Singer-Songwriter  Nicole Atkins is a singer-songwriter who Bruce Springsteen once said possesses "a truly singular voice."   Think Roy Orbison's "Cryin' if he were a woman. The orchestral sweep of the wall of sound, the bleak vision of Nick Cave and Leonard Cohen; the darkly mysterious girl group-on-acid musings of Julee Cruise and Lynch composer Angelo Badalamenti; the sorrow of Patsy Cline, the rock-a-ballads punk of The Pretenders, and the '60s psychedelia of Love and Nuggets. All with a redeeming sense of hope amidst the emotional wreckage...   She most recently opened for Stevie Nicks on her latest arena tour, as well as headlining a series of Nashville Residencies.    She just finished recording her new album "Drama" with producer Pat Sansone of Wilco. Featuring members of her band, Dr. Dog and the Texas Gentlemen, it will be released this fall on Chris Isaak's new imprint, Wicked Game via the legendary Sun Records.

Rock Early on, The Clarks were once described as a bunch of out of tune country hicks. The band took irreverent offense to the comments, saying they were never country hicks. Eleven studio albums and a couple of guitar tuners later, The Clarks have struck again with Madly In Love At The End Of The World, a lively ride down a rural lane, laced with love, mourning, and questions about where it all goes from here.    The band recorded the eleven song album in a converted A-frame church, which helped highlight a series of warm, rock and Alt-Country tunes. The four original members, Scott Blasey (vocals, acoustic guitar), Rob James (6 and 12-string electric guitars, vocals), Greg Joseph (bass, vocals) and David Minarik (drums, vocals) are joined by fellow touring mates Gary Jacob, Skip Sanders and Noah Minarik, tossing out some spanking pedal steel, Hammond organ and tasty guitar. "We've never had more fun composing and recording songs," says bass player Greg Joseph.

Party/Cover | Rock Totally Taboo is a 4 piece NEPA Party rock band consisting of Joanna Bea - vocals Mike ET aka The Alien - guitar, Walker Loveland - bass & Joey Madara - drums. They have embodied the spirit of classic rock, alternative, funk & pop. With a captivating energy that is undeniable, they have seamlessly woven their talents together, revisiting iconic tunes that have shaped the landscape of modern music. Their repertoire is a time machine of sound, transporting listeners back to the rhythmic decades of the 60's 70's, 80's, 90's & 00's. Every performance is a melodic journey, with each song a chapter, and every note a memory that guarantee an experience that resonates with all, young and old.   For those yearning to revisit the golden days of rock and pop, or those curious to explore its evolution, Totally Taboo is your ticket to a musical odyssey like no other. Imagine Lady Gaga fronting Led Zeppelin and you begin to get an idea of what you have in store!

Party/Cover The Wallenpaupack Area Middle School Modern Band is a five-piece, student-led ensemble under the direction of Mr. Ryan Kozich. This group performs a wide range of music, including pop, funk, musical theater, and rock and roll, while regularly rotating instruments.  Students in the ensemble develop skills on multiple instruments, including guitar, bass guitar, keyboard, and drum set, and each student performs as a lead vocalist on at least one song. The group presents at least two performances each school year.  This ensemble has performed at the 2026 PMEA All-State Modern Band Showcase at the Kalahari Convention Center and has been selected to perform at Musikfest 2026 in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, in addition to their regular school concerts.

Indie Julia Melito is a soulful singer-songwriter from Bucks County & the Lehigh Valley. She has been writing music her whole life, cataloging experiences with heartbreak, grief, joy, and love through her songs. She weaves spellbinding melodies to capture the mood of her lyrics. When she is not onstage, you can find her counseling high school students or pondering life on the bank of her favorite creek.

Country | Oldies | Polka Joe Stanky & the Cadets music has given birth to 6 CD's featuring originals and cover songs. One of their original songs, "Polka Dancer," was featured on a Grammy-winning recording by Brave Combo. In addition, the new Indie film by Jason Sherry entitled "Cubes" includes the music of Joe Stanky & the Cadets. Perhaps their most famous tune is "The Dancing Mouse," which as inspired thousands of fans to get up and dance. Their albums include, Ridin' High, The Switch Is On, Heart Moving Melodies, Fightin' Side, Band for Hire, and their most recent, Sing Me Back Home. The band has traveled from PA to Florida to Texas, Virginia, Conn., NJ and NY. the band plays it all from polka to country to modern and rock. They also appeared at Gilley's in Pasadena, Texas. The band website, www.joestanky&the Cadets has more info on the band and pictures.

Street Performer Lucy Juggles' one-woman comedy juggling show is a unique blend of southern charm, extraordinary stunts, and emotional vulnerability. Full of bad jokes, cute tricks, unicycles, and lots of knives, Lucy's Denim & Danger Circus show will delight audiences of all ages!

Street Performer Twisted Rhythm, an award-winning show based out of Clearwater Beach, Florida. We combine high energy breakdancing with signature comedic stylings through nonstop, laugh-out-loud moments and jaw dropping moves that leave the audience on their feet.

Rock Vodou is a band from Lehighton, PA founded in 2025. They fuse 70s psychedelic rock with the likes of soul and improvisation. Their inspirations are vast, spanning from Theater to the Blues. Vodou is a nine-piece band comprised of: Rowan Schaeffer - Vocals Aiden Wowk - Keys Jed Cordes - Guitar Zane Zimmerman - Bass Guitar Elijah Pagotto - Saxophone Carson Hauser - Trumpet Nolan Strauss - Trombone Kian Bowman - Drums Adam Gardiner - Drums

Folk | Americana | Indie | Pop | Rock Stacy Gabel is an award-winning singer-songwriter, recording artist, and dynamic live performer originally from the Lehigh Valley, PA. She tours nationally and internationally as a solo artist and featured vocalist in major productions, most recently the rock orchestration, Attack on Titan: Beyond the Walls world tour. Known for her versatility and original music, her songs-including fan favorites "Sunny Days," "Straight to Voicemail," "Stir Crazy," and "One Perfect Summer"-have been featured on radio, TV, commercials, and charted internationally. She performs locally with her jazz-pop ensemble, Stacy Gabel: A Touch of Class, and The Stacy Gabel Band. This year at Musikfest, Stacy appears in a solo show on Main Street, with Stacy Gabel: A Touch of Class at Liederplatz, and as "Miss Stacy" in her interactive touring children's show. She also works bi-coastally in TV and film as a writer and actress in Los Angeles, with several exciting projects currently in development.

Alternative | Rock | Post Hardcore Famous Prophets are a six high school students (soon to be college students) who formed a band in 2023. The band consists of guitarist Jack Sanderson, drummer Eamonn Dacey, guitarist Luca Bamabci, saxophonist Eric Harding, trumpeter Olivia Gennaro, and bassist Matthew Stebbins. They produce a unique sound taking inspiration from a mix of alternative rock, post hardcore, and post punk.

Alternative | Punk Cold Start is an Alternative Punk Rock band from Gloucester MA on the North Shore of Massachusetts with strong community values focusing on bringing people together. The band pulls from a wide range of inspiration, spanning many different decades and genres such as Metal, Country, and Jazz. Each of their songs conveys a story, presented through a pop-style approach with the band's hard-hitting instrumentals.

Folk | Americana | Indie littlebird duo is Sarah Lanier & Carl Palmeri of littlebird & the bad eggs, in a scaled down version of their eclectic, anthemic 9-piece ensemble.    Based between the Lehigh Valley and Poconos in PA's Slate Belt, the full band (Chris Condel/drums, Claire Anderson/trumpet&vocals, Kristi Weaver/cello, Bjarni Nermoe/electric&double bass, Wendy Wilson/violin, Carson DeAngelis/keys, and Eve Lanier/vocals) spent late 2025 and early 2026 in preproduction for their debut album, recorded in spring 2026 at Cako Studios. Their original music spans the wide array of the individual member's styles, all supporting the dramatic, theatrical compositions of the band's primary songwriter Sarah Lanier.

Party/Cover Your favorite cover band Starting Tomorrow delivers your favorite hits from the past....today! Formed in 2016 in the Lehigh Valley, the band has become a staple in the live music scene, performing rock, alternative, country, and pop covers from the 1960s to the present. Known for their unique style and energetic performances, they incorporate mashups and meta-covers into their sets. Starting Tomorrow has been a featured act at Musikfest from 2017 to 2024, including headline performances at Plaza Tropical in 2023 and 2024, and an appearance at Festplatz in 2022. Their dynamic and eclectic setlists have graced big stages, festivals, tailgates, bars, pubs, parties, and weddings.

Alternative | Rock | Original Grunge Lone Till Payday is an Alt Rock collective from Northwest NJ that has 2 vocalists- 1 male & 1 female that create a dynamic push and pull that is captivating and confrontational. Fueled by raunchy guitars and unfiltered emotion, LTP's innovative sound is built on contrast-- melody and chaos colliding. Their music moves between tension and release, raw power and haunting hooks which is both volatile and deeply human. Their collective efforts are what sets them ablaze delivering raw energy with depth, creating music that hits hard, lingers long, and refuses to be predictable.

World Ten Tiny Dances is a performance series dedicated to fostering innovative dance and providing an accessible performance experience for a diverse audience...all on a 4-by-4 foot stage. This edition will feature Banat Al Sahra, a vibrant community performance group founded by Carmen Garrison.

Blues | Folk | Americana | Rock Jonah Lloyd (guitar, vocals), Brian Lally (bass, mandolin, guitar, vocals) and JP Wasicko (percussion) perform open-ended versions of Americana, rock, and singer-songwriter classics. Their style is improvisational at times with hints of bluegrass and country. Due to their dynamic interplay, no two shows are ever the same.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Country Folk | Americana Pop Adeline Csontos is a 16 year old singer-songwriter from Allentown, Pa. She has over 50 original songs. Her authentic acoustic style blends heartfelt originals with current and timeless cover songs. She creates a performance that is both captivating and inspiring. With angelic vocals and melodic guitar playing, fans of great music will enjoy cherished memories, and the splendor of a live performance.

Blues | Jazz GT3-Jazz! Is a Lehigh Valley band based in Hellertown. We support the talents of more than 2 dozen fabulous local musicians with more than 150 gigs each year. You'll find us every Tuesday & Saturday at The Wooden Match and regularly at Historic Hotel Bethlehem, Legends Cigar Bar-Easton, and many other local venues.

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Alternative | Pop | Rock Zoey is pop/rock artist from Lancaster known for her distinctive vocals and rare ability to deliver soulful performances while playing bass guitar. A recipient of *Best New Artist or Band* at the Central Pennsylvania Music Awards, Zoey has quickly emerged as one of the region's most compelling new voices.   She performs as the front woman of Zoey and the Iconic, a dynamic four-piece band blending emotionally rich songwriting with warm, guitar-driven arrangements. Zoey's musical journey began at age eight with classical violin, laying a foundation in composition and performance that continues to shape her artistry. At fifteen, she transitioned to the bass guitar and began performing out in venues. With Zoey on bass and lead vocals, the group creates a sound that balances grit, heart, and authenticity-resonating just as strongly on stage as it does on record.

Classical Named for the Erinyes (a.k.a. the Furies) from the Greek tragedy Oresteia by Aeschylus, and with roots in Estonia, Lithuania, Greece, the United States and Finland, the Erinys Quartet was founded in 2018 at the Sibelius Academy in Helsinki where they worked closely with cellist Marko Ylönen. Since Autumn 2025 Erinys has been the graduate string quartet-in-residence at Indiana University Jacobs School of Music in Bloomington, IN (USA).    Erinys Quartet was awarded the First Prize at the 2025 Trondheim International Chamber Music Competition in Norway. At the Bad Tölz International String Quartet Competition in 2023 they received the Audience Prize, as well as the Esterházy Foundation Special Prize. They also won the Bronze Medal at the 2024 Fischoff Chamber Music Competition.   Since 2021 Erinys Quartet has been supported by Le Dimore del Quartetto where they are also a part of the European Union-sponsored MERITA platform.

Funk | R&B | Soul   PAULA BRION started her singing career through a organization named Central Brooklyn Talent Search (C.B.T.S) founded by Congressman and Senator Major Owens. She credits them for keeping her safe from the Harsh Brooklyn streets. She loved to sing! When asked to sing with a band by the C.B.T.S's organizer Barbara Barnes, she didnt hesitate to join as their lead female vocalist! This band took her to places that she could have never imaged at age 14. The band named "KINGDOM" performed and became one of Brooklyn's well "LOVED and REQUESTED" bands. After years of opening up for named artists like Delphonics, Ray, Goodman, and Brown, Stylistics, just to name a few. The band performed many cover songs. She was encouraged to do more and become a singer with an original. This began my solo career.    PAULA BRION was introduced to a new genre of music called "DANCE/HOUSE" Music. It was a music that allows the VOICE to be free.

venue: Lutron Jazz Cabaret Stage at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Blues | Jazz | Modern Jazz Multi-instrumentalist, Dan Wilkins returns for a second year with his piano trio, featuring bassist Joe Michaels and drummer Hugh Kline. Wilkins who may be better known for his saxophone playing has led groups at Musikfest for a decade plus of performances. The trio will perform original music from their 2025 release 'Oceans Beyond the Sky', reimagined music from jazz canon and new material written this year. The trio performs in the Lehigh Valley and surrounding areas, such as Deer Head Inn, Vic's Jazz Loft, Orange Cafe, Flemington DIY, and regularly at Yianni's Taverna. Wilkins performs on August 2nd in the same location at 8 pm with his saxophone and quartet. Come enjoy the immersive theater experience at the Frank Banko Alehouse Cinema inside Artsquest.

Children's/Family For over 15 years, A Storybook Party has been delighting families across Eastern Pennsylvania with unforgettable, high-quality character experiences that transform ordinary moments into extraordinary memories.   Founded on a love of storytelling and live performance, A Storybook Party specializes in bringing beloved fairytale-inspired characters to life through interactive entertainment. From enchanting princesses and heroic princes to adventurous mermaids, fairies, and superheroes, each character is carefully crafted to create an authentic and immersive experience for children and families alike.   As a locally owned and family-run business, A Storybook Party is deeply connected to the Lehigh Valley community. They take pride in supporting local events and organizations, frequently partnering with schools, libraries, and community initiatives to bring accessible, family-friendly entertainment to a wide audience.

Alternative | Folk | Americana | Pop "Leighann & Company" was started in 2017 after veteran musician, Leighann Burke, had a vision to create another form of music that would astound audiences. She began working closely with the incomparable guitarist and vocalist, Andrew Jon Sleboda, which led to the formation of an amazing musical bond. Leighann's powerful vocal range and abilities leave people speechless. Her bubbling on-stage personality, and her ability to connect with audience members, instantly warms a room. Leighann has been performing since 2001 as a lead vocalist and front person. Aside from managing and performing in "Leighann & Company", she has performed nationally in multiple cover bands and tribute shows such as: the 80's tribute band, "M80"; "David Stone: The Johnny Cash Experience"; "Y.M.I."; and "123GO!". Andy's guitar abilities, along with his soothing voice, paves the way for melodic masterpieces. Andy is also a veteran musician and has performed nationally and regionally since 1994.

venue: Wind Creek Steel Stage at PNC Plaza
description: with special guest George Pippen Since their formation in 1994, multi-GRAMMY Award-winning, diamond-selling band Train has sold more than 10 million albums and 30 million tracks worldwide, amassing over six billion global streams. Train's climb to the top began in San Francisco, as the original 5-member band tenaciously built a loyal hometown following, leading up to their debut self-titled album released by Columbia in 1998. The tumbling wordplay of “Meet Virginia” gave them their first unlikely radio hit, and 2001's Drops of Jupiter broke them to multi-platinum status. Known for their critically acclaimed catalogue of chart-topping hits including #1 best-selling smash “Hey, Soul Sister, double-GRAMMY-winning hit “Drops of Jupiter,” 5x platinum-certified anthem “Drive By,” Train has had 14 songs on Billboard's Hot 100 Chart and 13 albums on the Billboard 200 Albums Chart, six of which reached the Top 10, including Drops of Jupiter (2001), My Private Nation (2003), California 37 (2012), Bulletproof Picasso (2014),For Me, It's You (2016), and a girl a bottle a boat (2017). For over three decades, Train has been captivating audiences across the globe with their signature blend of rock and pop, infectious melodies, and heartfelt lyrics. With an extraordinary career that includes three GRAMMY Awards, two Billboard Music Awards, multiple diamond/platinum/gold citations, and dozens of other honors, Train continues to reach new milestones, boasting two tracks, “Hey, Soul Sister” and “Drops of Jupiter,” that have reached RIAA Diamond status and surpassed one billion streams on Spotify. Partners  Media Partner

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Country | Oldies | Rock  A multi-genre music duo performing for all ages, this duo seamlessly blends elements of rock, country, oldies and R&B to create a unique sound that's both eclectic and captivating. The duo features Kristen Morgenstern whose powerful, soulful vocals range from Patsy Cline to Janis Joplin and Chris Gates guitarist with his versatile, masterful guitar work. Kristen is the winner of The Morning Calls Readers Choice Award for "Live Entertainment" 2023, 2024 and 2025. She has been on Music Monday, WFMZ, and nominated for an internationally recognized country music award. Kristen performs locally and in the tri-state area, Florida and Nashville. The duos dynamic and charismatic stage presence makes for a truly unforgettable performance.

Alternative | Rock Poppycock Babble is an American rock band hailing from the Lehigh Valley, Pennsylvania. Formed in late 1995, they took the local music scene by storm with an explosive sound that was equal parts whimsical melodies and raw, rock 'n' roll energy. They toured extensively throughout the northeast until 2006 playing some of the biggest clubs in NYC and Philly. They gained an impressive and fiercely loyal following and quickly became one of the area's premiere original bands from that era, winning multiple LVM Awards in the process. Known for their intense and interactive live performances, they've shared stages with the likes of Fuel, Rusted Root, Big Wreck and Isle of Q delivering their driving sound to thousands of music lovers. 2026 marks the 30th anniversary of the band's performance career and what better way to celebrate this landmark occasion than a prodigious Poppycock Babble production at Musikfest. Catch ya' on the flipside! ;-)

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks

description: Alternative | Party/Cover | Rock American Vanity turns modern rock into a mirror...vivid, unfiltered, and built to reflect what it feels like to live in your own skin. Led by dual vocalists Virginia Franks (bass) and Luke Hoffman (guitar), with Luke Vanchure on lead guitar and Carter Dennis on drums, the four-piece creates music that takes aim at how image, approval, and identity get tangled in the attention economy, where everyone's chasing views but no one feels seen.   Drawing from the depth of alt-rock with punk and art-rock influences, American Vanity delivers a sound that's contemporary, intentional, and distinctly their own. The musicians behind American Vanity are no strangers to national touring or industry recognition, having previously performed across the country as Burn the Jukebox.    Now stepping into a new era as American Vanity, the band is preparing to release its first full-length album with Grammy-nominated producer Matt Squire (Panic! at the Disco, All Time Low, Ariana Grande).

Alternative | Rock Fronted by singer-songwriter Billy Bauer and accented by the otherworldly tones of electric violinist Chris Lorenzetti, Billy Bauer Band is rounded out by Richie Ares (drums) and Warren Snead (bass). Together, they blend the musicality and groove of contemporary jam bands with the storytelling and accessibility of heartland rock.   The band's sound has earned them appearances at some of the largest festivals and stages across the country, building a reputation for energetic, musicianship-driven performances.   Since forming in 2006, Billy Bauer Band has opened for nationally touring artists including Third Eye Blind, Phillip Phillips, Tim Reynolds (Dave Matthews Band), O.A.R., Young the Giant, Blues Traveler, Gin Blossoms, and Pete Francis (Dispatch). Their music has also been featured by ESPN, Disney, Six Flags, SuperCuts, Sam Ash Music Stores, and Radio 104.5 Philadelphia.

Alternative | Indie | Singer-Songwriter Alana Mars is a queer alternative pop musician based out of Bethlehem, PA. With her holy trinity as guidance (Lana del Rey, Florence + The Machine and Stevie Nicks) Alana's conversational lyric style feels like you are reading directly out of her diary (and in some cases you are) while her complimentary guitar playing helps to paint the picture and bring it all to life. Prior to moving to Pennsylvania, Alana had a decade-long career in Denver, CO performing at local venues and festivals as well as being featured on local radio stations like Indie 102.3 (Colorado Public Radio) and KTCL 93.3. Along with multiple national tours and numerous music festivals under her belt, Mars released her debut EP "The Prologue" in May 2024 which is available on all streaming platforms.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Folk | Americana | Indie Cass Noelle is a Lehigh Valley native with a powerful and angelic voice whose strength as a songwriter comes from her ability to set to music her perseverance through hardship and finding hope where it feels hopeless. Audiences are often moved and connect with her music as it helps them feel that they are not alone.   With influences like Brandi Carlile, Jewel, and the Cranberries, her songs leave an enduring imprint on listeners.    Her EP, Joy Will Come, is available to stream on all major platforms.

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Folk | Americana | Indie | Pop Alexa Tabbacchino is a 24 year old singer, songwriter, multi-instrumentalist, producer, and audio engineer.    With help from her sister, Tabbacchino got involved in music during middle school, where she participated in choir and the musicals. During college, Tabbacchino began performing acoustic covers at local restaurants. In 2024, she began performing her original music.   Tabbacchino grew up in south New Jersey playing baseball, swimming in the ocean, and secretly writing songs in her bedroom. Written, produced, and recorded over the course of 3.5 years, Tabbacchino's debut EP Evergreen is out now. The EP dives into some of her most life changing experiences so far: the passing of loved ones, self discovery in the motherland, and overall 20's existentialism. The sound of Evergreen has been described as contemporary folk with a pop flair. Tabbacchino is drawn to authenticity and women paving the way in the music industry.

Children's/Family | Folk | Americana Dave Fry has been entertaining Lehigh Valley audiences for decades with his unique skills as a performer at festivals, schools, folk clubs and bars, and senior centers. He has numerous CDs for both families and adults, appearances on PBS-TV programs and NPR radio shows, and is particularly known for his work as a Teaching Artist in schools across the Northeast.    He is the only performer to have performed at every Musikfest in Bethlehem.   Dave has been honored by the State of New Jersey as a Master Artist and by the State of Connecticut as a Teaching Artist for his work in schools. Dave received the Distinguished Service Award from the NJ Governor's Awards in Arts Education Program in May 2022.   Dave is an award-winning folk radio-programmer, with 45 years of on-air experience on WSAN, WMUH, WLVR and now on WDIY with Sunday Folk and Live From Godfrey Daniels.   Dave co-founded Godfrey Daniels in 1976 and it remains one of the premier folk music clubs nationally and internationally.

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Alternative | Indie | Rock Joy and the Wildfire is a high-voltage indie rock band with big hooks and a magnetic edge. Led by powerhouse vocalist Samantha Joy Pearlman, the NYC-based band blends gritty storytelling with arena-ready anthems, drawing comparisons to icons like Debbie Harry and Paramore. Come for the music. Stay for the curls.   "...a band to watch out for [and] inevitably fall in love with" - CLOUT Magazine  "A Seismic Shift in the Indie Rock landscape" -MusicEarShot.Com  "A voice like if silk and fire had a baby"- Illustrate Magazine  "Electrifying Storytelling and a Fearless Approach to Rock"- RotateMag

Funk | Rock Meet the Mayor, the feral offspring of funk and Musikfest. Born and raised on gyros and corn, baptized in a plastic mug. Free yourself to our musical jibberish Monday, August 3rd at 7:30pm.

Jazz | Pop | R&B | Soul CRAIG KASTELNIK AND FRIENDS has been exciting audiences at festivals, concerts and club venues for the last several years. This special edition of the group appears at Bethlehem Musikfest, Musikfest Cafe at the ArtQuest Center, Bethlehem, PA, Monday, August 3, 2026, 7:30 - 9 PM (Free concert) to perform their magic with a soulful mix of Pop, Funk, R&B and Jazz Grooves. Highly acclaimed keyboardist and vocalist CRAIG KASTELNIK has performed / recorded with such artists as Eddie Rabbitt, Bernard Purdie, Randy Brecker, Phil Woods, G.E. Smith, Bob Dorough and Simone among many others. His unique approach to the Hammond organ / keyboards along with his soulful vocals are powerful features of the group. PAT FLAHERTY, on vocals and percussion puts her signature style on every song she sings. Pat and Craig also form the musical duo "KATO". The band also features PETE FLUCK saxophones and harmonica, JIM MEYER on guitar, DON PLOWMAN on drums and special guest ROB STONEBACK on trombone.

Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Folk | Americana | Indie | Pop Luca Diadul creates introspective, vulnerable music to inspire acceptance in heartbreak. His journey started in the mountains of Japan, where, too tired to care who was listening, he sang for a handful of strangers in a tiny hostel and realized how deeply performing music mattered to him. Luca's music centers on soulful songwriting and a tender, powerfully decadent vocal style. He prioritizes narrative dynamics, lush vocal harmonies, and a casually elegant levity that balances more emotional songs. The result is a nostalgic and soothing energy that invites you to drift and dream.

Country Folk | Americana | Honkabilly Ithaca, NY-based Drew Kiddoo and The Blackouts (pronounced kih-doo) have been crafting their distinctive brand of all- original country music for nearly a decade. Drawing inspiration from the golden era of American music, they fuse 60s honky-tonk and classic country with a gritty rockabilly edge-creating a sound that's as twangy as it is dark. Drew's songwriting delivers a timeless, easy-to-sing-along nostalgia, electrified by an aggressive energy that fuels barn-burnin', good-time dance crowds. Their songs tell stories of the open road, heartbreak, and endless nights, captivating audiences while touring throughout the nation.

Alternative | Indie | Singer-Songwriter Alana Mars is a queer alternative pop musician based out of Bethlehem, PA. With her holy trinity as guidance (Lana del Rey, Florence + The Machine and Stevie Nicks) Alana's conversational lyric style feels like you are reading directly out of her diary (and in some cases you are) while her complimentary guitar playing helps to paint the picture and bring it all to life. Blues | Rock | Original Project 22 is a collaboration of independent recording artists and award winning musicians out of Eastern Pennsylvania who've played night clubs, festivals and honky-tonks from Memphis to Maine. Project 22 brings the audience an eclectic blend of soulful and high energy Roots, Rock, R&B, Contemporary Blues and Originals that gets everyone up on their feet.   P22's Line-up consists of...  - Dana Gaynor - Guitar & Vocals - (Dana Gaynor Band) - Glenn Kaucher - Guitar & Vocals - (Rough Mix) - Dennis Wieand - Keyboards & Trumpet - (BC Combo, Charlie Brown's Notorius Groove and The Kim Brewer Band) - Doug Brown - Bass - (Friar's Point Band & Little Buddy Band) - Brian Berlanda - Drums & Vocals - (Friar's Point Band & Little Buddy Band)   P22's High Energy Delivery and Musicianship was formed in the light of our influences, to the likes of The Allman Brothers Band, Blackberry Smoke, Joe Bonamassa, to name a few. You will hear those influences throughout their performances.

Party/Cover It is a little-known fact that most Amish practice a tradition similar to the Christian "Confirmation" and Jewish "Bar Mitzvah" called "Rumspringa" (literal translation: "running around") in which Amish children at the age of 16 have an opportunity to live free of the strict Amish code of conduct before deciding if they want to come back and be baptized into the Amish church. Surprisingly, virtually all of the youths return. But sometimes they don't. Four out of the original six members of The Amish Outlaws were born and raised in Lancaster, PA and had a strict Amish upbringing. No electricity from land lines, no alcohol, no musical instruments, but their spirits were too wild for the Amish lifestyle. Once they trampled in the Devil's Playground, there was no turning back. In the years since Rumspringa, the members of the Amish Outlaws have become very well acclimated to the pleasures and vices of the modern world... music, most of all.

artist: We May Be Right: A Tribute to Billy Joel
venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Pop | Rock | Tribute We May Be Right brings their acclaimed Billy Joel tribute show back to Musikfest! Experience all your favorite Piano Man classics delivered by a 5-piece band of musicians who combine their individual touring experiences to create an unforgettable live performance! You'll find yourself singing and dancing along to the songs that shaped (and are shaping) your lives. You may be crazy if you don't check out We May Be Right...

Surf Rock | Psychedelic | Garage Sun Bus Started in the winter of 2020 as a collaboration Between Multi- Instrumentalists Egan Miller and Erik Santana. With interests in Surf Rock, Psychedelic and Garage Rock as well as retro exotica, and 60's pop.   SUN BUS is: Erik Santana-Guitars Egan Miller: Guitars Grace Young- Bass Zach Martin: Keys, Auxiliary Percussion and guitars Jesse Braun: Drums

Tribute | Jam Band Shakedown Citi is a fresh new force on the jam band scene, born in the heart of a Grateful Dead parking lot at Citi Field in 2023. When the band's members crossed paths on Shakedown Street, something clicked - and the music hasn't stopped since. ​ Rather than imitating the Dead, Shakedown Citi channels their spirit - creating spontaneous, soulful performances that feel alive, adventurous and in the moment.    Each set is a journey, full of unexpected twists, deep grooves and musical conversation that pulls even the most skeptical listener into the vibe. Every Shakedown Citi setlist is thoughtfully and meticulously crafted from the Dead repertoire, affiliated catalogs and beyond, balancing deep cuts and beloved anthems, rarities and reprises. No two nights are ever the same - each performance stands as a work of art on its own, alive in its moment, while also fitting into the larger patchwork of the repertoire.     It's not just a tribute - it's the Dead, reborn.

artist: Rubix Kube The Galaxy's Most Original '80s Tribute Band
venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Pop | Rock | '80s For 19 years, RUBIX KUBE has taken the universe by storm as the most original '80s tribute band of all time!    From their NYC headquarters to U.S. troops overseas, in the most prestigious venues across the globe and galaxies far, far away . . . "The EIGHTIES STRIKE BACK Show" is a one-of-a-kind, high-energy, ever-evolving, interactive '80s extravaganza, with a proven track record of electrifying crowds of all ages and demographics. With an unmatched word-of-mouth history, their shows are legendary.    The pro and versatile musicians, of RUBIX KUBE personify your favorite '80s stars, include DOZENS of costume changes by SEVERAL lead singers, '80s props, videos, balloons, confetti-even BUBBLES!  Out on the road you may come across many inferior copycat '80s acts, but don't be Fa-fa-fa-foolin! Be sure to jump in the DeLorean with RUBIX KUBE, because "where we're going, we don't need roads!" This is the most AWESOME '80s experience to ever exhilarate your senses.    This is RUBIX KUBE.

Alternative | Pop | Rock Fresh out of the studio with their new single and video "Queen of Saturday night" Dear Anna is a restored freight train coming your way. Combining rock, indie and a tinge of Nashville, Dear Anna has honed their sound by performing 75 to 100 gigs per year for the past decade. Heartfelt vocals, guitar tone to die for and a bass-drum combo that is tighter than a... (you fill in the blank).   With the unique ability to discern the audience, every Dear Anna show is tailored to entertain the crowd in front of them. Stocked with a huge repertoire of covers and originals, their entertainment possibilities are endless! Dear Anna thrives on making each show a unique, living, breathing experience.   Dear Anna THE band you need to catch. With a flick of a switch they can transform to Party Mode and make you dance and sing the night away!

Party/Cover | Pop | Rock Attention Switch is a powerhouse rock band made up of musicians ages 9-11 who are already turning heads in the music scene. The lineup features Coen Krysiak on guitar, Quinn Krysiak on bass, Paden Wilkosz on drums, and Nora Schultz on guitar and vocals. Despite their young age, Coen and Quinn have already built an online following of over a million fans across their social media platforms.   Formed in 2024, Attention Switch has wasted no time making their mark-playing shows up and down the East Coast and winning new fans everywhere they go. Guided by their mentor Ryan Krysiak of the band First To Eleven, Attention Switch combines raw talent, high energy, and a passion for rock that proves age is no barrier when it comes to making great music

Alternative | Indie | Rock Jenny Founds is a Bethlehem-based multi-instrumentalist and singer-songwriter known for her dynamic, diverse performances. She began playing jazz clubs at age five in a duo with her mother, moved into professional theater in middle school, and later toured internationally with the School of Rock All Stars, sharing stages with Jon Anderson (Yes), Slash (Guns N' Roses), Perry Farrell (Jane's Addiction), and members of Frank Zappa's band. After earning her degree in musical theater from Marymount Manhattan College, Jenny settled in Bethlehem and formed the original rock trio Ruby Dear. She currently tours internationally as bassist and vocalist with Start Making Sense: A Tribute to Talking Heads, while also teaching at School of Rock Easton. Jenny is currently working on her debut solo album.

Country | Oldies | Polka Dave Betz has been performing his own style of music that he covers both locally and in many different states across the country for over 45 years. He surrounds himself with the most talented and lively musicians that you can put on a stage bringing a fun experience to the crowd. Dave loves to entertain and put smiles on the faces of the many people he has met and looking forward to meeting many more along the way. Dave plays the Accordion and does most of the lead vocals this is a show you don't want to miss.

Street Performer Twisted Rhythm, an award-winning show based out of Clearwater Beach, Florida. We combine high energy breakdancing with signature comedic stylings through nonstop, laugh-out-loud moments and jaw dropping moves that leave the audience on their feet.

Street Performer Lucy Juggles' one-woman comedy juggling show is a unique blend of southern charm, extraordinary stunts, and emotional vulnerability. Full of bad jokes, cute tricks, unicycles, and lots of knives, Lucy's Denim & Danger Circus show will delight audiences of all ages!

Bluegrass | Folk | Americana | Rock Emerging in 2025 from Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, After August blends folk, bluegrass and singer songwriter into a genre-defying sound.   After August is a sophisticated bluegrass duo from Bethlehem, PA built on the heart and soul of music. From classic covers, to brand new originals and all the zany stuff in between, you're sure to have a beautiful time with these musicians. Andi and Ella have both been solo acoustic performers since 2019. Having met in high school for music and music production, the two quickly bonded over one epic concert and shared music taste, inspiring them to create a sound of their own. The duo is eager to chase the euphoric energy of music and in turn, share it with people who are just as creative and adventurous. Join them in jamming on tunes from the 60's to present day, and explore the depths of their minds with their originals.

Alternative | Folk | Americana | Indie Zach Russack - musician based out of NW, NJ.   Portrayed in his music is the purity of innocence and the harshness of truth. For someone whose music is largely characterized by still introspection, Zach Russack doesn't really slow down. His music, which broaches many subjects and is often marked by intricate acoustic guitar and his soft vocal style, chronicles his reflections on the dichotomies in his life: Growing up in a small town and performing in a big city or building a secure foundation to start a family while pursuing professional goals in the music industry, just to name a few. Whatever the challenge, on whichever side of the coin, Russack's music and performances illustrate he has the versatility to handle them all. Following the release of his second full-length record, Russack continues to shape his world through melody, reflection, and an unflinching passion for connection.

Jazz | Pop | R&B | Soul | Rock Joe Grisanzio is a Jersey Shore-based singer, songwriter, and producer. Characterized by genre-bending and passionate live performance, Joe and his band travel throughout the Northeast for performances at festivals, small venues, and private events. Joe has foundations in pop, rock, and jazz, citing Amy Winehouse and Adele as influences to his style of writing. Joe is a member of ASCAP, and BMI.

Alternative | Pop Gina Royale is a New Jersey-based alt-pop artist known for emotionally charged lyrics, powerful vocals, and raw vulnerability. Her music has reached over 1.5 million streams across 160+ countries, building a growing global fanbase.   A dynamic performer, Royale has toured extensively across the U.S., playing shows and festivals from New Jersey to Texas and throughout the East Coast, including Boston, Maine, Pennsylvania, and New York.   Beyond the stage, she's made waves in sync. Her single "Low" opened the film After Everything, and in 2025, her song "Mr. Lennon" was featured in A Love Letter to the Beatles. She has also secured deals with major sync libraries DeWolfe Music and Exit Strategy Productions.   Whether on stage or on screen, Gina Royale creates music that resonates with honesty and lasting impact.

Country | Rock Erin Kelly's skilled guitar playing and powerhouse vocals define her Country and Rock roots. She has opened up for major artists including Hunter Hayes, Aaron Lewis, and Frankie Ballard. Performing in stadiums including Madison Square Garden to Citizens Bank Park, she has captivated audiences with both her original music and covers. Erin's rendition of the National Anthem has been heard at major and minor league stadiums across the region. Her experience in performing the National Anthem gave her the privilege of singing for General Colin Powell and President George W. Bush. Erin continues to work with producer Rich Herring of the Little River Band on her original music.

Party/Cover | Pop | Rap/Hip-Hop Avenue 16 is a Party Cover Band! Our sound is driven by a tight thumping danceable rhythm with dynamic electric guitar melodies.  Avenue 16 incorporates vocalists to produce a crisp, harmonic sound that keeps crowds energized. Our secret weapon is original hip hop artist, CL who packs a flavorful punch throwin down verses in common pop tunes, freestyling to get the party jumpin' and bringin' some ol' school hip hop back to today's mix

Folk | Americana | Indie Eliza Rush is a Canadian-born, Pennsylvania-based singer-songwriter whose music blends folk, Americana, and modern pop into something deeply personal and immediately compelling.    Classically trained and shaped by years of performing experience, her songs name the things people feel but struggle to say - honest, lived-in, and rooted in real experience.   Onstage, she is backed by seasoned regional musicians who bring her songs to life through dynamic arrangements anchored by the rich vocal harmonies that have become a hallmark of her live performances.   Earlier this year, SiriusXM's Director of Music Programming discovered her music and reached out directly - resulting in 12 songs placed across seven channels including The Pulse, The Blend, Coffee House, and The Spectrum.

Party/Cover | Rock The Swell is a Bethlehem, PA-based cover band led by brothers Travis & Tanner Brown, trading lead guitars & vocals. Raised on their parents' vinyl collection and their drummer dad Andy's basement jam sessions, the brothers grew up surrounded by sound.   During the pandemic, the Browns turned lockdown into a nightly underground workshop, sharpening their chops and building a varied set list that blended their musical tastes. Once restrictions lifted, Steve Millheim joined on bass, and The Swell officially took shape. The lineup later expanded to include Eric Rusack (formerly of The Riffters) on guitar, bass and vocals.   What began with basement videos that caught fire on social media soon evolved into packed live shows. The Swell delivers a dynamic mix of classics from The Beatles, Hendrix, Stones, Bowie & the Eagles to the modern sounds of Green Day, Foo Fighters, Blink 182 and nearly everything in between. Elvis, Chuck Berry & Johnny Cash also make appearances in The Swell's sets.

Country | Pop Kendal Conrad is a singer-songwriter and social media influencer. She has performed a duet onstage with Keith Urban and has opened shows for 50+ country artists including Blake Shelton, Alabama, Kane Brown, Chris Young, and more. She has also performed the National Anthem for 7 NBA teams, 15 MLB teams, and 2 NFL teams as well as the 2025 Military Bowl. She is on a mission to sing the National Anthem 250 times and at all 30 MLB ballparks. Notable anthem performances include the Philadelphia Eagles at Lincoln Financial Field, Chicago Bears at Soldier Field, Los Angeles Lakers at Crypto.com Arena, NCAA Wrestling Championships at the Wells Fargo Center, and the Milwaukee Brewers Wild Card game televised on ESPN. She is also a social media influencer and has amassed a cumulative following of 50,000+. IG and TikTok: @kendalconrad

description: Country | Rock When I'm not helping clients navigate their taxes and Medicare benefits, you can usually find me behind a guitar. I'm a part-time musician who loves playing classic country and rock covers, bringing a little extra rhythm and joy to the community both on and off the stage.
