description: Alternative | Indie | Rock Post fusion-core reactors "Wallace, Tonight!" were born in the basements of Philadelphia and emerged as staples of the scene. Frequenting venues such as Milkboy, Ortlieb's, and Kung-Fu Necktie, the band is coming in hot off their 5th EP release "Your Kids Are Invited to a Birthday Party," OUT EVERYWHERE MAY 1ST! Stay Hydrated!

Celtic | Irish | Folk | Americana Pennsylvania-based Celtic-tinged folk rock collective River Drivers has a unique passion-infused style of music that draws from Americana, Celtic and Appalachian influences and features powerful and distinctive vocals and multi-instrumentalists. The high energy music of Kevin McCloskey (guitar, vocals and mandolin), Mindy Murray (guitar, vocals and banjo), Marian Moran (Irish whistles and concertina) and Meagan Ratini (fiddle and Irish flute) explores pervasive themes of hard-working people and social justice and their repertoire strikes a fine balance of originals and obscure folk songs, resurrected from deep folk vaults. Their music has garnered acclaim from major international Celtic and folk music journals including No Depression, fRoots and Irish Music Magazine. Their songs have been featured on the website of the UMWA, and have won numerous international awards including the 2025 International Songwriters Day Award and the International Women's Freedom Song (2020-2026).

Classical | Jazz | Pop Monocacy Brass is a brass quintet rooted in the heart of the Lehigh Valley, bringing together musicians whose collective experience spans decades of performance and artistry. Their repertoire reflects an eclectic musical spirit, ranging from the elegance of Baroque masterworks to the energy of pop rock, offering audiences a dynamic and engaging concert experience. Beyond the quintet, the members of Monocacy Brass are involved in the regional music community - serving as educators, leading ensembles, and performing across a wide variety of groups. Personnel: Keegan Lerch & Alec Rubery, trumpet; Rena Hummel, French horn; Ben Ashton, trombone; and Andrew Lynn, tuba.

As a locally owned and family-run business, A Storybook Party is deeply connected to the Lehigh Valley community. venue: Lutron Jazz Cabaret Stage at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Bluegrass | Celtic | Irish | Jazz Steve Walker is an accomplished vocalist and multi-instrumentalist known for vibrant live performances of Jazz standards, Celtic & Irish Jigs, holiday classics and his own humorous original songs about life in the Lehigh Valley. His energizing and captivating shows have been featured at Musikfest, Christkindlmarkt, Berks Jazz Fest, Philadelphia Folk Festival and many concert stages throughout Southeastern Pennsylvania and beyond. He is the featured harmonica player on a nationally released recording on the Relix label, jamming with members of the Grateful Dead and New Riders. Winner "Best String Instrument Player" at the Lehigh Valley Music Awards, Steve sings and plays guitar, mandolin, harmonicas, banjo, bazouki and more with an infectious happy beat. Steve's warm stage presence and exquisite tone invite the audience to get their toes tapping, faces smiling, and hearts singing.

venue: Wind Creek Steel Stage at PNC Plaza
description: with special guest PUDDLES PITY PARTY Weird Al brings his legendary full-production multimedia comedy rock show back to the concert stage with the BIGGER & WEIRDER 2026 Tour, playing his iconic hits as well as some never-performed-live-before fan favorites. Al's long-time band is joined by four additional players to create a super-sized concert experience. “Weird Al” Yankovic is the biggest-selling comedy recording artist in history. A 5-time Grammy Award winner, he is best known for his parodies of the biggest musical artists over the last 4 decades, including Madonna, Michael Jackson, Taylor Swift, Queen, U2 and Lady Gaga, all with their personal blessings. His many hits include “Amish Paradise,” “Eat It,” “Like a Surgeon,” “Smells Like Nirvana,” “Word Crimes,” and the platinum-selling “White & Nerdy.” His last album Mandatory Fun is the only comedy album in history to debut at #1 on the Billboard Top 200. Yankovic's live shows have entertained audiences across the globe for generations, including appearances at such iconic venues as Radio City Music Hall, The Hollywood Bowl, Red Rocks, Carnegie Hall, Wolf Trap, The Kennedy Center and Madison Square Garden. In 2022, Yankovic produced and co-wrote the Emmy-winning biopic WEIRD: The Al Yankovic Story, starring Daniel Radcliffe in the title role. VIP Meet & Greet and Backstage Tour Packages are available for this show. Click here for more information on VIP packages. Special Guest: PUDDLES PITY PARTY Puddles Pity Party, the 7-foot sad clown whose golden voice is “comparable to any Grammy winner” (Los Angeles Times), has amassed over 900K YouTube subscribers and performed sold out shows around the globe including San Francisco's Palace of Fine Arts, London's Soho Theatre and a residency at Caesars Palace in Las Vegas. He has performed on stage with such legends as Eric Idle, Nick Offerman, Jack Black, Maynard James Keenan and Primus. Puddles' one-of-a-kind “textured voice laced with melancholy” (NY Times) has been hailed as “operatic” (Boston Globe) and his show both “life-affirming” (Herald Scotland) and “hysterically funny” (LA Weekly). Blues Folk | Americana | Alt-Country Dina Hall is a performing songwriter and recording artist, rooted in Bethlehem, Pa. She blends folk, rock, blues, and alt-country into a dynamic amalgam of Americana music, featuring her resonant, familiar voice on each song. Hall has solidly established a decades-long career as a performing songwriter, honing her craft in intimate listening rooms and bringing that spark to large theater stages and music festivals. Hall's songwriting and performance can be reminiscent of the enigmatic storytelling of Dylan and Springsteen, combined with the wildly feminist passion of Melissa Etheridge and Brandi Carlile, and the powerful whispers of Emmylou Harris and Tracy Chapman.   "...she blew me away...vocally, she has a second gear to her voice." - Mike Greenblatt, The Aquarian   "...a roaming Americana spirit, a raw rootsy rock strut, and an introspective folk storytelling quality." - Tom Hilton, Aldora Britain Records   "So Good." - Aubrey Plaza, American actress, comedian, and producer

description: Indie | Pop | Rock Bethlehem local Ellery Collins crafts every part of her songs from writing to recording. Joined by a backing band this year, she's bringing her music to life with more depth and energy than ever before. Celtic | Irish Folk | Americana Rock The Muckers are a five-piece folk-rock band from Atlanta. description: Funk | Rock | Progressive Rock Brooklyn-based Moon City Masters blend funk, prog-rock, and classic rock energy into a modern sound that's both virtuosic and groove-driven. The group has completed multiple U.S. and European tours and performed aboard The Rock Boat festival cruise.

Rock | Indie Easy Honey is a Charleston, SC based indie rock band that infuses its singer-songwriter folk roots with an original mix of nostalgic East Coast surf-rock.    Originating at The University of The South: Sewanee over a cooler filled with freshman year college punch, the dynamic of this group is one of their strongest elements, existing with an electricity and a true sense of comradery both on and off stage.    The tremendous creative power between the band's members Selby Austin (vocals, guitar), Darby McGlone (vocals, guitar), Charlie Holt (drums, vocals) and Webster Austin (bass, vocals) is on full display in their versatile new EP, Plaid (out April 30 via Third Brother Records), which they wrote and recorded over a three-day session at an isolated cabin in snowy Marble, Colorado.

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Rock | American Roots Making Movies is the Latin Grammy-nominated group led by Enrique Chi - born in Santiago, Panamá, and raised in the American Midwest - forging a sound guided by tradition, yet unmistakably its own. The band has been described by Rolling Stone as "an eclectic blend of rumbero percussion, delicate organs, and grungy fuzz," while NPR calls the project "perhaps one of the most unique groups around today." Live performances are anchored in the rich rhythmic traditions of Panamá and delivered with the force of a psychedelic rock show. At the center is Chi's performance on the mejorana, a rare Panamanian folkloric instrument woven into a modern psychedelic context and pushed through distortion and reverb.

venue: Lutron Jazz Cabaret Stage at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Blues | Funk | Jazz Artisan Blue Band is a power house quartet featuring relentless improvisation and top notch song writing. Their sound is rich in traditional Blues, Funk, Jazz and and R & B. They put on a live show that captures the audience and sets new highs. description: Indie | Pop | Rock Sophie Rice is young singer-songwriter and guitarist from the Lehigh Valley. Her interest in music has been apparent in so many aspects of her life since childhood but she started prioritizing writing and performing her own songs as a creative outlet around two years ago. This is her second year playing musikfest and she hopes to soon release more of her own music online!

Alternative | Indie | Rock Attack Dog is the premier rock outfit from Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. Punk leaning yet genre defying, the sounds and sights of their vigorous and animated live performances elicit Black Flag and the Grateful Dead. Their debut record, "Remastered 2009," released via homegrown label Pray for Rain, has brought them on escapades across the great American Midwest, Northeast, South, and Canada, with countless shows in hometown Philadelphia. Nurtured by Philadelphia's vivid DIY scene, Attack Dog has honed their craft by playing hundreds of basements, bars, ballrooms, cabins, kitchens, roadsides, museums, and mausoleums; sharpening their knives to draw blood at the very touch. Lyrically clever, sonically thunderous, physically erratic, and socially well-bred, Attack Dog is the unstoppable force that meets an immovable object.

Celtic | Irish | Folk | Americana The Rogue Diplomats are an Irish-American Pub Band based in both New York City and Bethlehem, PA. They perform the spirited melodies and lyrical story-telling of traditional Irish jigs, shanties, drinking songs and ballads, with a dash of contemporary American folk songs and original compositions, all delivered in close vocal harmony and with charmingly irreverent humor.

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Party/Cover It is a little-known fact that most Amish practice a tradition similar to the Christian "Confirmation" and Jewish "Bar Mitzvah" called "Rumspringa" (literal translation: "running around") in which Amish children at the age of 16 have an opportunity to live free of the strict Amish code of conduct before deciding if they want to come back and be baptized into the Amish church. Surprisingly, virtually all of the youths return. But sometimes they don't. Four out of the original six members of The Amish Outlaws were born and raised in Lancaster, PA and had a strict Amish upbringing. No electricity from land lines, no alcohol, no musical instruments, but their spirits were too wild for the Amish lifestyle. Once they trampled in the Devil's Playground, there was no turning back. In the years since Rumspringa, the members of the Amish Outlaws have become very well acclimated to the pleasures and vices of the modern world... music, most of all.

description: Pop R&B | Soul Hayley is a Lehigh Valley-based musician and singer-songwriter known for her warm vocals, relatable songwriting, and engaging live performances. She performs regularly throughout the Valley at bars, restaurants, wineries, and private events, connecting with audiences through thoughtfully chosen covers and her original music. A returning performer at major ArtsQuest events, Hayley is appearing at Musikfest for the fifth time, in addition to being featured at Palette & Pour, solidifying her place as a trusted and recognizable voice in the area's music community. A guitarist since the age of 12 and a lifelong vocalist, Hayley draws inspiration from artists like Adele and Amy Winehouse. She brings a distinctive pop, soul, and R&B-influenced style to her performances, delivering unique interpretations of popular songs with passion and authenticity. Stay updated on Hayley's performance schedule by following her on Instagram (@hayleyjackson_) and Facebook (Hayley Jackson Music).

artist: Robbie Lawrence & The Steelworkers Polka Band
description: Old | Polka | World Formed in 2018 as a way to honor the hardworking European immigrants who settled in South Bethlehem, PA during the late-19th and early-20th centuries, local radio & TV personalities Robbie Lawrence and Alex Fydryszewski made it their business to keep high-quality polka music alive and well in the Lehigh Valley region for years to come.    Flanked onstage by former members and longtime veterans of the polka world, rest assured - polka fans old and new will have a blast keeping Musikfest's roots alive and well under the Service Electric Festplatz tent! Street Performer | Juggler | Comedian The Flaming Ginger is a fast paced comedy and juggling show. From ground to sky he brings the laughs and backs them up with skills and thrills. Winner of both national and international performance competitions, he will quickly become a highlight of your day.

Street Performer The Cate Great Show combines high-level circus and original comedy into a sharp, witty, big-hearted show that plays to all audiences. She is an award-winning twenty year veteran of circus, stage, and street. Trained at the Quebec City Circus School she's performed in over 11 different countries including: Australia, New Zealand, Dubai, Italy, and the UK as well as most of the US and Canada.

Alternative | Folk | Americana | Indie Tim Harakal is a Lehigh Valley based pop/folk singer/songwriter. Early in his career, Tim cut his teeth in a thriving college coffee house scene and quickly found an affinity for thoughtful lyrics and complex melodic finger-picking melodies.  After gaining a local following, Tim began opening for larger national acts at opera houses and state theaters and eventually would put out his first album, which he promoted on an East Coast tour as a supporting act for Trevor Hall! This lead to a 3 year music filled journey in Los Angeles where he played historic rooms such as the Viper Room and The Hollywood House of Blues to name a few. Tim is now back in the Valley working on new music and frequenting some of the areas well known venues and festivals.

artist: Lehigh Valley Charter High School for the Arts Contemporary Music Ensemble
description: Alternative | Party/Cover | Rock The Charter Arts Contemporary Music Ensemble is an ensemble that performs music from rock/pop mediums from all eras of the 20th and 21st centuries! The ensemble is made up of both vocal and instrumental students from 11th and 12th grade at the Lehigh Valley Charter High School for the Arts.

Country | Pop | Rock Melody Cruz is a Lehigh Valley based singer-songwriter whose music blends emotional honesty with polished pop sensibility. Her journey began at age three performing alongside her family at Musikfest, and by her teens, she was already a regular on regional stages. In 2021, Melody released her debut single "Toolate" earning her features on WFMZ 69 News, Fox 29, and multiple televised music segments. Her original music has been showcased at Musikfest, sold-out Vic's Jazz loft solo shows, and festivals across PA and NJ, both solo and with a full band. Known for vulnerable lyrics, powerful vocals, and a commanding stage presence, Melody creates songs that feel deeply personal. Whether performing originals or weaving them into live cover sets, she brings soul and connection to every audience she meets.

Country | Party/Cover | R&B | Soul This powerhouse group blends classic rock, country, funk, and R&B into an unforgettable mix of danceable tunes. Get ready to move to the rhythm, whether it's a soulful groove or an all-out rock anthem.

Country Caryn Dixon delivers a Country/Americana-fueled sound that feels like a backroad in the mountains, pulling listeners through the peaks and valleys of life. Her live show moves from boot-stompin' energy to quiet, tear-filled moments, carried by heartfelt storytelling. At the core of her artistry is a mission to be a voice for women navigating domestic abuse, toxic relationships, and healing. Her radio single Bird in a Cage tells the story of a woman escaping abuse and recently broke into the Country Radio Breakout Charts, inspiring women worldwide. Hailing from central Pennsylvania, Caryn began writing at seven, blending influences like Merle Haggard and Patsy Cline with modern storytelling. With over 400K listeners and 1.6 million YouTube views, she continues to build a loyal audience through honest, emotionally driven music.

Alternative | Rock   Julia Miralles is a rising rock and indie artist from the Philadelphia area, known for her powerful vocals, emotionally direct songwriting, and commanding stage presence. Blending influences from rock, blues, and even metal, she brings a raw, "old soul" quality to her music that resonates well beyond her age.   Raised in a musical household, Miralles was immersed in music early on-especially through her father's band, whose rehearsals and jam sessions helped shape her artistic identity. Inspired by artists like Joan Jett and Fiona Apple, she began writing poetry before transitioning into songwriting and guitar performance.   She first gained attention with her debut single "I Am Your Summertime," released at just 15, which quickly built an online audience. Since then, she has released multiple singles-including "Dream of You," "Just One Minute (What a Waste)," and "This House" - showcasing a mix of heavier rock energy and introspective lyrics.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Indie | R&B | Soul Sarah Harrington is a Lehigh Valley-based vocalist and songwriter known for her soulful, haunting, and powerful voice. Classically trained and comfortable across multiple genres, her musical background spans music theater, indie, gospel, and soul.   She performs locally as one half of the duo Cathartic Hearts alongside musician Rich Jeffreys, where the pair are known for their tight harmonies and intimate performances throughout the region.   Sarah has released nationally distributed recordings that remain available on streaming platforms, reflecting a long history of songwriting and recording that continues to evolve. Her current singer-songwriter work explores a more personal sound rooted in emotional storytelling.   Originally from Syracuse, New York, Sarah comes from a long line of female singers, and music has always been a deep source of connection and healing in her life. She now lives in the Lehigh Valley where she is also a proud mom of three.

The pair has been performing together since their early childhood, fostering a deep musical connection that infuses their interpretations with warmth and synergy. Drawing on a shared foundation in classical traditions, they explore a diverse repertoire that highlights the expressive interplay between cello and guitar.

Blues | Rock Zaire plays rock whether it be classic rock like The Beatles or Tom Petty, to newer rock like The Killers or Radiohead. Zaire has a unique blend of strong 3-part harmonies.    Zaire has been playing the Lehigh Valley area for 15 years at restaurants, bars, festivals and private events.

venue: Lutron Jazz Cabaret Stage at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Jazz | R&B | Soul The Frost Duo is comprised of pianist and guitarist Christian Gratz and vocalist/pianist LeahBeth Evans. The Frost Duo is well known for their versatile musical styles which include composition and live performance in the style of Jazz, r&B and soft rock The Frost Duo has performed across the United States. The Frost Duo gained popularity within the jazz community in 2024 with their release of "Saturate," A smooth jazz composition that can be found on apple music's "Smooth jazz cruise" editorial playlist.    The Frost Duo has been heard on BBC radio, Itunes Jazz charts and BDS/Neilson Most Added. They have worked with artists such as Oli Silk, Thom Rotella, David Shaver and Adam Hawley. They have opened for artists such as Louis Prima Jr. and the Whitnesses and Jill Sobule.   The Frost Duo looks forward to sharing their passion for music including jazz on the Cabaret Stage at Musikfest this year!

Country | Pop | Rock Formed in 2018 in Allentown, PA as an acoustic cover project, DMC DUO has been performing in Pennsylvania and surrounding states ever since. DMC DUO began working on original material in 2021, and debuted their first song, Loved You Anyway, in March 2022 beginning their journey as an original music artist. Original music continues to be created, and released, with new music currently being prepared to drop soon. DMC DUO's sound includes a country style mixed with rock and pop.    Darrell Adams and Marisa Dawson have been seen performing individually in various bands and duos for many years. After meeting each other while both filling in for the same band, and realizing there was a great chemistry between them, they they decided to join forces to create the successful duo that they have today. Do not let the name fool you, as DMC DUO also performs as a trio, and full band.   The duo has had the privilege of sharing the stage with notable acts such as Orleans, Chris Kirkpatrick (NSYNC).

description: T.I. Aside from his contributions in entertainment including, 10 hit albums, multiple Grammy's, Billboard BET and American Music Awards, countless blockbuster movies and top-­rated television shows including, ATL, VH-­1's T.I. & Tiny: The Family Hustle, American Gangster and Marvel's Antman, to name a few, Tip “T.I.” Harris has made an indelible mark on the world of business. He is an artist, entrepreneur and activist who has amassed a business empire to include a record label, a 10-­year old clothing line (Akoo, which stands for “A King of Oneself”), a real estate holdings company, production company, non-­profit organization (Harris Community Works), co-­owner of the global streaming service, TIDAL, arts and museum curator, investor and much more. Harris served on the Mayor of Atlanta's Transition Team, where he focused on policy and legislation;; and currently serves on the Reimagining Atlanta Task Force, aimed at creating restorative practices and a community-­driven approach to addressing incarceration. He has been recognized most recently as a thought leader at the United States Capitol and during the Operation Hope and Forbes Magazine business conferences. His community focus is around driving economic mobility and equity in vulnerable, underserved communities. Harris was also awarded the first-­ever “Voice of the Culture” award by ASCAP and is the host of a new #1 rated podcast, expediTIously with Tip “T.I.” Harris.  DaBaby Charlotte, North Carolina, native and multi-Grammy Award nominee DaBaby began his career in 2014, quickly building buzz in his hometown that fueled his rise to national recognition. His debut project “Baby on Baby” spent 55 weeks on the Billboard 200 and featured the breakout hit “Suge,” while his first official album “KIRK” debuted at No. 1 and included the smash single “BOP.” He continues his chart dominance with “Blame it on Baby,” as “Rockstar” featuring Roddy Rich spent seven consecutive weeks at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 and achieved multi-platinum success, earning multiple Grammy nominations. Known for his standout collaborations, DaBaby helped propel remixes like “Levitating” by Dua Lipa and “What's Poppin” by Jack Harlow to the top of the charts. In 2022, he teamed up with YoungBoy Never Broke Again for the joint project “Better Than You,” followed by solo releases including “Baby on Baby 2” and the EP “Call Da Fireman,” whose focus track “Shake Sumn” gained momentum with a remix featuring Sexyy Red. With more than 6.4 million global streams, 22 billboard Hot 100 entries in a single year and recognition including the XXL Freshman Class, a cover feature with Billboard and appearances on “Revenge of the Dreamers III,” DaBaby continues to expand his impact as both an artist and CEO of his label Billion Dollar baby Entertainment, most recently releasing his fifth studio album “BE MORE GRATEFUL” and solidifying his position as a prominent figure in music and culture. description: Folk | Americana | Indie Korina is an independent folk artist and singer-songwriter best known for her charming melodies and evocative lyrics. Korina draws upon folk, jazz, classic rock, and pop to create her innovative melodies, but is most notably inspired by legendary songwriters Joni Mitchell, Elliot Smith, Bob Dylan, and Paul Simon. Korina crafts deep vulnerability and relatability in her music and looks forward to connecting with fellow artists and future audiences.    Be sure to check out her recent releases, including her single "You'll Never Really Say It" and latest EP, "Ghosts in My Room."

description: Folk | Americana | Pop Clover Stieve (formerly CLOVER) is a singer-songwriter from Frenchtown, NJ blending the genres of folk, pop, and soul. Inspired by the likes of Lake Street Dive, Maggie Rogers, and Carole King, she puts the energy of the 60s and 70s in conversation with contemporary music creating a sound that resonates across generations. From energetic and explosive groove-driven songs that get you on your feet, to songs that bring you to your knees in tears, Clover touches on topics of love, grief, empowerment, and spirituality. Her debut EP Atlas, recorded at Electric Lady Studios, was released in May of 2025 and her full length LP is set to release in 2026.

Children's/Family | Folk | Americana Lolly Hopwood is a kindie artist that can perform as a solo act, a duo, trio, or full band. She is based in Doylestown, PA. Her interactive children's music performances are filled with imagination and movement that will really get the audience involved in the show. Using props like parachutes, pom poms, scarves, bubbles, and puppets, the audience won't just be watching the concert, they will be a part of the show.   Lolly's musical influences are classic Sesame Street, Mister Rogers, Harry Nilsson, Cat Stevens, Elizabeth Mitchell, Dan Zanes, Dolly Parton & an array of modern indie artists. She has been compared to Elizabeth Mitchell & the Laurie Berkner Band.

Like a junkyard bathtub full of homemade instruments on sparkly pink roller skates, The Hot Mamas combine grit, humor, harmonies and sass as their vehicle taking audiences on madcap adventures. Their writing styles range from jazzy to pop, from funky punk to murder ballads, soulful blues to drinkin'-when-you're-sad country tunes. The Hot Mamas are quirky, fun, profound and sincere and not to be missed!

Funk | Jazz | Pop The Wonton Soups are a quirky and lovable horn band from Bethlehem, PA. Blending experience from jazz, musical theater, marching band and self taught musicians, The Wonton Soups have a truly unique sound influenced by funk, ska, swing, and big band. Expect to hear fresh approaches to covers that land with all age demographics.

Folk | Americana | Indie | Rock Guitarist, producer, and singer-songwriter Coby Siegel is a Washington, D.C. based artist focused on narrative-driven contemporary music influenced by jazz language and folk-rock sensibility. His work prioritizes songcraft, improvisation, and collaboration, and places a premium on live performance as a means of connecting with his community and peers. Coby's recorded work includes his debut single Jupiter, his EP In the Meantime, and contributions as a sideman on the self-titled EP by D.C. based band Strutman Lane. His creative output spans original songs, instrumental compositions, poetry settings, and improvisation, balancing deliberate composition with improvisational flexibility. As a bandleader, Coby has presented original music at festivals and venues along the East Coast, including Musikfest, Muhlapalooza, and DC9. He is an active collaborator across several ensembles, including Strutman Lane, Onyx, and the 229 Army Band, and has appeared on major stages such as the 9:30 Club.
