description: Folk | Americana | Indie | Jazz TEMMA writes music she hopes is as nourishing for others as it is for her, finding new perspectives through every song. She weaves together diverse inspirations from indie folk, jazz, pop soul, and Yiddish music in her songwriting. TEMMA's soaring vocals and expressive performances-honed through classical voice and musical theatre training-breathe life into her intimate, personal compositions. Her debut EP, Searching for the Sky, is now available on all streaming platforms.   She has been writing songs since she was 7 years old and performing in English and Yiddish since she was 3, appearing on 5 continents. She was featured in a documentary film, "When Our Bubbas and Zeydas Were Young: The Schaechter Sisters on Stage," which was presented at film and music festivals in Miami, Montreal, and Toronto. These days, TEMMA performs around the Lehigh Valley and NYC.

artist: Stacy Gabel: A Touch of Class
description: Jazz | Pop Stacy Gabel: A Touch of Class delivers a smooth, stylish take on your favorite songs, reimagining modern hits and beloved classics with a sophisticated jazz-pop flair. Fronted by the charismatic Stacy Gabel, the ensemble blends elegance and energy, weaving in swing, soul, and inventive arrangements that charm and engage audiences. Stacy leads top-tier musicians to create musical moments that are timeless and unforgettable. She also tours internationally throughout the year as a solo artist, with The Stacy Gabel Band, and with a vocal ensemble in a symphonic rock concert inspired by a globally acclaimed animated TV show. A voting member of the Recording Academy, Stacy brings professionalism, passion, and sparkle to every performance.

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Pop | Retro Pop The Queen of Retro Pop has arrived. Currently on tour and best described as "Norah Jones meets Nancy Sinatra", Tabitha Meeks' feel-good throwback music will make you bop your head, tap your toes, and feel empowered all at once. Her piano solos and live shows are unmatched in fun vintage energy, and her catchy, light hearted songs will be stuck in your head for days. Her single "Ready for Christmas" had a viral moment with over 1 million TikTok & Reels and 3 Billion Views.

Blues | Rock James Supra Band. High energy, harmonica driven blues/rock band. Playing a wide verity of covers and original music. Featuring Chris London on guitar. Brian Berlanda on drums and Doug Brown on bass.

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Alternative | Rock The last few years have been a little hazy for Lilly Hiatt, who finds herself searching for answers on her striking new album, Forever. Recorded at home with her husband, Coley Hinson who produced and played most of the instruments, Forever grapples with growth and change, escape and anxiety, self-loathing and self-love. The songs are intensely vulnerable, full of diaristic snapshots and deeply personal ruminations, but they're also broad invitations to find yourself in their unflinching emotional excavations. The result is a raw, unvarnished work of love and trust that walks the line between alt-rock muscle and singer/songwriter sensitivity, a bold, guitar-driven exploration of maturity and adulthood from an artist who wants you to know you're not alone, no matter how lost you may feel.

artist: Derek Warfield & The Young Wolfe Tones
description: Celtic | Irish Folk | Americana A special group of Irish men and women have been responsible for preservation of the tradition of Irish ballad singing over the past 50 years. Derek Warfield is a distinguished name among them. Derek Warfield and They Young Wolfe Tones are bringing that musical tradition forward with a reverence for Ireland's past and the energy and optimism of a new generation of Irish musicians. Derek Warfield & The Young Wolfe Tones are thrilled to be celebrating 18 years On the One Road and are delighted to be celebrating it in style with the release of a new CD/Vinyl "Let The Free Birds Fly" released in June 2022, and performances all over America including some great Irish American Festivals & Venues. Please check www.theyoungwolfetones.com for all of our latest tour dates.   "We're presenting an old tradition in a new package - and I've the same passion for it now as I had when it all started 50 years ago!"

artist: Burt Bacharach Goes Latin with Clave Legacy Quintet
venue: Lutron Jazz Cabaret Stage at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Folk | Americana The Crybabies are an acoustic duo who blend guitars and voices playing classics from Laurel Canyon, Big Pink,The Brill Building, and Abbey Road along with some catchy originals. They'll "Put sweet sadness in your ear!"

Alternative | Party/Cover | Rock Twisted Times is a cover band based out of Clinton, New Jersey, serving genres ranging from hard rock to country and everything else in between. With deep alternative and classic rock roots, our passion emanates in songs ranging from the '60s, all of the way to the 2000's. Formed in 2016, we continue to expand our setlist and improve our sound to make a one-of-a-kind experience for every crowd. Consisting of Kyle Weed (Vocals, Guitar), Jacob Bettelli (Lead Guitar, Vocals), Tommy Gassaway (Drums), and Tommy Mcgourty (Bass), we have an unwavering passion for entertaining and captivating our audience all while playing songs that everyone can sing along to.

Party/Cover | Pop | Trop Rock Grab your beach chair, your suntan oil, and your favorite drink, and get ready for a trip to the islands. For more than 25 years, Jimmy and the Parrots have been playing to delighted crowds all over the United States and as far a south as St. Kitts in the Caribbean and as far north as Ontario in Canada! One of the most requested Jimmy Buffett cover bands in the country, Jimmy and the Parrots perform not only all the great Buffett songs you love, they also play new and classic rock 'n roll, as well as outstanding original songs written by lead singer/guitarist Jimmy Maraventano. Their cover songs include favorites by the Beach Boys, Zac Brown, Jerry Jeff Walker, Harry Belafonte, and Bob Marley, among many others. The band truly appeals to all ages and all musical tastes.

Party/Cover | Pop | Rock Garden State Radio has been one of New Jersey's most popular cover bands well over the past decade. Known for their unique live show that feels more like a stadium concert than a local cover band, they have performed thousands of shows for huge crowds all over the East Coast. Their seasoned, professional musicians (including multiple lead vocalists) perform an eclectic variety of music spanning everything from the '70s to today. Garden State Radio specializes in creating an interactive entertainment experience that will keep your guests on the floor and having an incredible time!

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Rock | Tribute | Phish Rift is like a hoagie. Tasty, sometimes very greasy. Not a Wawa hoagie. Like, a really good hoagie from that local deli down the street.    It's the same 4 Philadelphians and a light guy playing the music of Phish, figuring out all that weird and beautiful music, and bringing it to the phans when the real band is out of town. Here we are, playing the same songs in the same places Phish played 35 years ago.    Rift has rallied a devoted following, performing 105 shows across the region , catalog of 100 Phish songs. We have taken the stage at venues including 118 North, Ardmore Music Hall, World Cafe Live, Elkton Music Hall, Anchor Rock Club, The 8x10, MusikFest, and the Newtown Theatre.

Rock Lou Franco has been a staple in the Philadelphia/Lehigh Valley music scene for over 30 years. He has performed and recorded with many artists over the years including Robert Hazard and Pretty Poison. Lou has shared the stage as an opening act for many notable artists, including Robert Palmer, Billy Ocean, Taylor Dane, Debbie Gibson, Bruce Hornsby, Stryper, and many others. Lou also leads a Jeff Beck tribute called TRUTH, that is one of only a handful of tributes to mastery of Jeff Beck. Quite a feat for any guitarist. TRUTH has been playing theatre shows to rave reviews over the last 2 years. Now, with Mitch Shelly on bass and Ray Cardona on drums the Lou Franco Project takes you on a journey of classic rock's greatest artist, from the Beatles to Van Halen, Pink Floyd, Jimi Hendrix to Jeff Beck this trio stretches the boundaries of improvisational jams leaving no stone unturned, with expert musicianship. This is a power trio you don't wanna miss!!

Pop R&B | Soul Hayley is a Lehigh Valley-based musician and singer-songwriter known for her warm vocals, relatable songwriting, and engaging live performances. She performs regularly throughout the Valley at bars, restaurants, wineries, and private events, connecting with audiences through thoughtfully chosen covers and her original music. A returning performer at major ArtsQuest events, Hayley is appearing at Musikfest for the fifth time, in addition to being featured at Palette & Pour, solidifying her place as a trusted and recognizable voice in the area's music community. A guitarist since the age of 12 and a lifelong vocalist, Hayley draws inspiration from artists like Adele and Amy Winehouse. She brings a distinctive pop, soul, and R&B-influenced style to her performances, delivering unique interpretations of popular songs with passion and authenticity. Stay updated on Hayley's performance schedule by following her on Instagram (@hayleyjackson_) and Facebook (Hayley Jackson Music).

artist: Experience Janis: A Tribute to Janis Joplin
description: Blues | R&B | Soul | Rock | Tribute CC Coletti channels the soul and spirit of Janis Joplin, known for her powerful vocals,  and dynamic stage presence, developed during her seven year tenure with, Meat Loaf's Neverland Express World Tours from 2003 to 2011. CC's powerhouse voice and electrifying stage presence, backed by her band of seasoned pros, TJ Jablonski (bass), Joe Cirotti (guitar), Dan Lieske (keys), Jamie Tiger (drums), Tony Perusso (trumpet), and Kevin Presutti (sax) (Bon Jovi, The Rascals, South Side Johnny and the Asbury Jukes, Bruce Springsteen, Gary U.S. Bonds) Experience Janis recreates the electrifying sound that defined a generation. CC and the band tear through Janis's classic, 60s sounds, by Big Brother and the Holding Company and Kozmic Blues Band performing hits including Me and Bobby McGee, Piece of my Heart and Mercedes Benz.

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks

description: Folk | Americana New Jersey Based Folk / Americana Band.  With a recently released album "Daydreams" released in November 2025. Recording in Nashville, TN Johnny Zarfati will be releasing new music throughout the year along with his 2nd album.

Alternative | Indie | Rock Lehigh Valley Girls Rock (LVGR) is dedicated to empowering cisgender girls and women + nonbinary and transgender folks through music and art. LVGR's mission is to create positive, nurturing spaces where people learn that their greatest power is being themselves, and we use music as the vehicle! LVGR is in its thirteenth program year and held its annual Get LOUD! Camp in July. Get LOUD! Camp is a week-long empowerment camp based on being in a band open to girls, trans, and nonbinary youth ages 10-17. Campers learn to play an instrument (no experience necessary), form a band, write an original song together, and then perform their song at a concert in just a week! In addition to making music, campers spend the week building friendships and attending educational workshops on self care, community building, and social justice. LVGR's performance features some of this year's campers and past program participants. We're thrilled to be at Musikfest to share the magic of our programs!

Pop | Rock | Acoustic Chaeli and Carrson are an acoustic duo centered on rearranging the songs that you love into a stripped-down acoustic set. They explore genres from rock to pop punk, adding their own unique feel to the classics. Some bands they are inspired by are Paramore, The Cranberries, Pierce the Veil, and Fall Out Boy. A love of music and performance brought this couple together and now they strive to share that growing passion for music with everyone who hears them play.

Polka | Pop | World Not your ordinary oompah band, Polkadelphia plays traditional German and Polish favorites as well as more modern covers by artists as diverse as Radiohead, the Beatles, Boyz II Men, and Metallica. Based in Philadelphia, they are in high demand throughout the area. Polkadelphia has been featured at Steelstacks Oktoberfest, Philly Bierfest, and Cannstatter Volksfest-Verein. They have performed on television at WVIA"s legendary Pennsylvania Polka, and have appeared as special guests on the reality tv show Worst Cooks in America.   Polkadelphia is made up of top Philadelphia musicians, with performers from such groups as the West Philadelphia Orchestra, Beatlemania Now, the Red Hot Ramblers, Jump City Jazz Orchestra, and the Violent Femmes.    Whether rocking out at the local biergarten, dancing the Fliegerlied in a tent at Oktoberfest, or getting your party started Polkadelphia style, they always bring the haus down.

Street Performer The Cate Great Show combines high-level circus and original comedy into a sharp, witty, big-hearted show that plays to all audiences. She is an award-winning twenty year veteran of circus, stage, and street. Trained at the Quebec City Circus School she's performed in over 11 different countries including: Australia, New Zealand, Dubai, Italy, and the UK as well as most of the US and Canada.

Street Performer | Juggler | Comedian The Flaming Ginger is a fast paced comedy and juggling show. From ground to sky he brings the laughs and backs them up with skills and thrills. Winner of both national and international performance competitions, he will quickly become a highlight of your day.

Blues | Folk | Americana | R&B  A former mattress salesman and warehouse manager, Jeiris Cook took a leap of faith into full-time musicianship at the age of 37-with a young family by his side. After countless late nights spent honing his guitar skills, a 2017 camping trip with his son became the perfect stage to test his newfound talent.   How does one go from mattress sales to troubadour? As Cook puts it, "I'd always sing in situations when it wasn't deemed appropriate-at work, during meetings, even on conference calls. Eventually, I realized the universe was knocking me over the head, trying to tell me something."   Several years later, Cook has performed across the New York Metro area in venues, restaurants, private events, and showcases. He also volunteers with Musicians On Call, an organization that brings the healing power of live music to patients in healthcare facilities. Deeply committed to music's restorative potential, Cook considers this work among his most meaningful contributions to his craft.

Classical | Pop | Rock CMS is a non-profit charitable organization that provides the inspiration and opportunity for anyone in our diverse community to achieve excellence in music education and appreciate the transformative power of music. Our professional teachers provide private lessons, group classes, and summer camps to students of all ages, from toddlers to seniors, in piano, strings, brass, woodwinds, percussion, voice, and recording technology; both in-person and online. Thanks to support from our philanthropic community for over 43 years, CMS has provided students in the Lehigh Valley with the life-long benefits of a quality music education and free performance opportunities. Located at 1544 Hamilton Street in Allentown, CMS offers 14 lesson studios, a state-of-the-art recording studio, and a unique 150-seat recital hall. A satellite studio is located in Reading at GoggleWorks. Learn more at http://cmslv.org/.

Alternative | Pop Hailing from Allentown, Pennsylvania, Seth Witcher is a dynamic creative force, exuding the essence of Emo-Core in his production, performance, and visual artistry. With soul-penetrating lyrics and melodies, Seth envisions his music as a guiding light through life's shadows, weaving an eclectic tapestry across Pop, Punk, Indie, Singer-Songwriter, and RnB.     In Seth's narrative, music transcends; it heals, empowers, and celebrates vulnerability. Though independent, he won a ticket to Hollywood Week this year on season 24 of American Idol, and has shared stages with 24kGoldn, Ryan Cabrera, robbietheused, Earshot, J.R. Richards, and The Orchestra.

artist: Lehigh Valley Charter High School for the Arts Latin Music Ensemble
description: Latin | World The Latin Music Ensemble at the Lehigh Valley Charter High School for the Arts is an elective ensemble in its second year that performs music that was developed and is culturally relevant to places from across the Caribbean and South America. The ensemble will feature music by performers such as Celia Cruz, Nelson Henriquez, Willie Colon etc.

Party/Cover | Pop | Rock Whiskey Therapy is a dynamic 5-piece party rock band from Southeast Pennsylvania. Renowned for their high-energy interactive performances, they captivate audiences with a diverse repertoire from the Isley Brothers to '80's classics, to today's rock and pop hits. Their unique approach blends top-tier musicianship with creative arrangements featuring compilations and medleys that engage and delight crowds of all ages. The band consists of Kevin Eppler (Guitar / Vocals), Sean Nester (Lead Guitar / Vocals), Gary Hayner (bass), Marshall Hoffritz (drums) and Bethlehem-native Pete Cheplick (keyboards). Debuting at Musikfest in 2025, Whiskey Therapy is thrilled to return in 2026 to the nations largest and best free music festival. We are planning to deliver an even bigger and better show at Festplatz this year you won't want to miss - we look forward to seeing all of you there!

Country | Folk | Americana Jamie Chesson is an emerging country/folk music singer/songwriter known for her captivating Angelic voice and heartfelt lyrics. Growing up in Florida & Kentucky, Jamie discovered her passion for music at an early age. Inspired by country legends like Dolly Parton, Alison Krauss, Trisha Yearwood & Shania Twain, she developed a unique blend of traditional and contemporary sounds.   With an innate talent for storytelling, Jamie began writing her own songs and honing her musical skills. Her dedication paid off when she started performing and captivating audiences in Nashville, Austin and Los Angeles with her authentic voice and genuine stage presence.    What sets Jamie apart is her down-to-earth personality, stage presence & ability to connect with the audience. As a former NFL cheerleader she thrives on stage in the spotlight.Jamie is currently performing In NYC, PA & NJ while she works on an upcoming album. She is excited to be apart of many music festivals and venues this summer.

Americana | Folk | Alternative Ethan Neal is a singer-songwriter from Barto, Pennsylvania, crafting songs rooted in simple, small-town storytelling. Blending elements of singer-songwriter, alt country, and folk, his music leans into honesty, atmosphere, and narratives drawn from the everyday. Influenced by artists like Bob Dylan, Jeff Buckley, Neil Young, George Harrison, Kings of Leon, Frank Ocean, and the tradition of old country music, Ethan places high value on the mundane and simple aspects of life-finding meaning in quiet moments, familiar places, and passing thoughts. His writing puts story first, favoring subtle detail and emotional truth over excess, while his live performances create an intimate connection with audiences. With music available on all major streaming platforms, Ethan is currently recording new material, continuing to refine a sound that feels both timeless and deeply personal.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Folk | Americana | Pop | Rock Ella Bruch is an acoustic musician from the Lehigh Valley, PA. With Ella, no two shows are ever the same. Each show is a unique, vivid, and engaging mix of everything from folk to hair metal, and of course her original songs. Ella has been performing for over 8 years and will be studying Music Business at Berklee College of Music. Before attending Berklee, she was a vocal major at the Lehigh Valley Charter High School of the Arts for 4 years. While she mainly performs solo, you can also catch her performing with her acoustic duo After August or with a band. Ella has been deeply rooted in the world of rock since she was born and her passion for it only grows. It's all peace and love around here!

Clindren's/Family Peggy Gernerd is Miss Maggie Sings! I sing, write, perform and record educational and fun music for children and their families. I started my children's music career by writing material for The Reading Public Library's annual summer reading program and have been writing similar shows since. I sing, play the guitar and ukulele and often incorporate one of a kind puppets (made by a local pupeteer who studied with Jim Henson's daughter). My shows are interactive: I encourage the audience to clap, sing and move to the music. I always incorporate stretching and dancing into my programs. My CD "Stay Healthy With Miss Maggie" was inspired by my two sons when they were little. I have performed at area elementary schools, festivals, public libraries, preschools, churches, museums and other events. This marks the third year I have performed at Musikfest. I am always ready to energize and inspire both children and their families to enjoy learning through music!

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Country | Folk | Americana | Rock Reservoir Hill is an original country band from Allentown, PA, formed in 2018 by singer-songwriters Dylan Flaherty and Brad Kramer. Blending modern country with Southern rock, folk, and jam-band influences, the band is known for honest songwriting and high-energy, crowd-engaging performances.   Their self-titled debut EP (2019) earned a Lehigh Valley Music Awards nomination for the single "Boneyard," followed by the full-length albums Desperate Times (2021) and Leave It All Behind (2023), highlighted by a headlining performance at the Levitt Pavilion in Bethlehem, PA.   The current lineup features Dylan Flaherty (vocals/guitar), Brad Kramer (vocals/guitar), Tommy Pogash (bass), Brian J. Anthony (bass), Abigail Zuckert (piano/vocals), Nygel Flaherty (rhythm guitar/mandolin/vocals), and Shohn Montano (drums). With four lead vocalists and a dynamic live show, Reservoir Hill continues to captivate audiences across the Northeast and beyond.

Alternative | Indie | Pop | Rock The ColourSound formed in 2004 and immediately began unleashing original tunes on the public. They started with a fan base from the Lehigh Valley and Pocono Mountains. But when they caught on within the New York City fashion industry, they eventually formed pockets of fans in cities across the nation.    As The ColourSound Acoustic Duo, Doug Batt and Sean Hieter not only thrill the masses with their acoustic takes on modern tunes, but they also bring on the nostalgia with the songs from the 70's, 80's and 90's that you forgot you liked.    Their eclectic mix speaks to all demographics and has allowed them to perform all over the country from Boston to LA, from Minneapolis to Houston and everywhere in between.

Folk | Americana | Pop Summer Harmony Men's Chorus is celebrating its 35th season in 2026, having been formed in 1991 when several men who shared a love for singing put their thoughts and voices together for what was to become the premier Men's Chorus of the Lehigh Valley. George Boyer, widely recognized throughout the Valley for his ability to meld vocal "instruments" was recruited to be its founding music director. In the years following, the chorus grew and matured not only in numbers, but also in its repertoire diversity, for its popularity in and beyond the Lehigh Valley, and for the quality of its musicianship. The Music Library ranges from the classics, sacred, Broadway tunes, barbershop, folk and a variety of patriotic standards. The chorus has appeared at many of the area's churches and other venues including Musikfest, and has performed the National Anthem for the Philadelphia Philliesand the Lehigh Valley Iron Pigs. Andrew Lutz-Long is in his fourth season as Summer Harmony's music director.

venue: Lutron Jazz Cabaret Stage at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Jazz | Gypsy Jazz C'est la Vie is a 4-piece Gypsy Jazz band from Bethlehem, PA, USA. Inspired by Romani guitarist Django Reinhardt and French swing violinist Stéphane Grappelli, they play Swing, Bossa and American Standards mainly from the 1920's and 1930's. The rhythm of C'est la Vie originates with the "la pompe" or percussive chugging of Ed Perenyi and Lee Daniels on guitars along with Mike Burns on upright bass. The melody is layered on top with leads by Bill Nixon on fiddle, Ed Perenyi and Lee Daniels.

Jazz | Pop | Great American Songbook The trio's vast array of musical influence and experience create a singular style and passion that endears them quickly to audiences. In addition to the multi-faceted ear-appeal with saxophones, keyboard, trumpet, clarinet, flute, harmonica, melodica and various percussion techniques, Dolcetto's (Italian wine-"sweet one") blend of Glenn and Deb and Jeff's vocal and harmonies is sure to please your musical palate. From jazz clubs and restaurants to weddings and corporate events, Dolcetto is always exploring new ways to create musical reviews of Standards, Smooth Jazz, Pop, Blues, Swing, Great American Songbook tunes, and even a little Dixieland.

venue: Wind Creek Steel Stage at PNC Plaza
description: with special guest Chandler Walters Hailing from Louisville, Illinois, multi-platinum singer-songwriter Bailey Zimmerman is one of country music's brightest stars, known for his chart topping hits and authentic gritty sound. After first gaining traction by posting original songs to social media in 2020, Zimmerman rocketed into the spotlight with the viral success of “Fall in Love,” which made him the fastest debut country artist to reach No. 1 on Billboard's Country Airplay chart in over a decade. In 2023, Zimmerman released his debut album Religiously. The Album. which arrived as not only the biggest streaming debut for a country album in history, but also the biggest all-genre streaming debut since 2021. The “comfortably bruising and appealingly bruised” (The New York Times) album also features his fourth consecutive No. 1 single “Where It Ends,” his 4x-Platinum, No. 1 debut single, “Fall In Love,” and its 5x-Platinum follow-up, the six-week No. 1 mega-hit “Rock And A Hard Place.” The album debuted at #3 on the Top Country Albums chart and #7 on the Billboard 200, instantly positioning him as one of the genre's most impactful new voices. Special Guest: Chandler Walters Bringing energy back to the classic-country tradition, Chandler Walters is a multi-talented native of Marietta, Georgia, signed to ERN's Cadillac Music / DeVille Records. Already a seasoned steel-guitar player and multi-Platinum, all-genre No. 1 songwriter, his burgeoning artistic career is now kicking off, schooled on a mix of rock and country influence. The guitar slinger moved to Nashville on a whim in 2021, picking up steel guitar and catching the attention of hitmaker ERNEST in the process, who promptly added Walters to his band. Traveling the world while learning song craft from the modern greats, he signed a publishing deal with ERN's Cadillac Music in 2024, earning monster success as co-writer on Post Malone's 5x Platinum, all-genre No. 1 single “I Had Some Help (feat. Morgan Wallen).” Walters posted six cuts on Post Malone's F-1 Trillion in total, and has also co-penned tracks for ERNEST (including “I Went To College, I Went To Jail” (feat. Jelly Roll)), Bailey Zimmerman (“Is This Really Over?”), Charles Wesley Godwin (“Dead To Rights”) and more. Boasting organic, live-show inspired country sonics (with plenty of steel guitar) and a knack for modern language, Walters' songs like “Come On Whiskey” find a proven hitmaker turning “real shit” into catchy, singalong anthems. Partners  Media Partner

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Blues | Jazz | R&B | Soul Velvet Salgado is a piano-vocal performer blending modern pop, jazz-influenced standards, and soulful originals into intimate, expressive sets. A U.S. Navy veteran, Velvet began performing publicly while in service, including singing the national anthem for command ceremonies. After leaving the Navy, she worked in professional theater in San Diego before returning to the Northeast, where she now performs throughout the NEPA and Lehigh Valley areas at private events, and curated dining experiences.   Known for her emotive delivery and conversational stage presence, Velvet creates performances that feel personal, warm, and immersive : whether reimagining contemporary favorites or sharing original music. Her work is rooted in storytelling, connection, and authenticity, making each performance feel both polished and deeply human.

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Alternative | Rock | Indie Slingshot Dakota, affectionately known as Slingy D, is a two-piece band from Allentown, Pennsylvania. Carly Comando and Tom Patterson craft a uniquely face-melting sound using nothing more than distorted keys and drums. Pulling from punk, hardcore, and pop influences, their songwriting and energetic stage presence make every show feel special.

Alternative | Rock | Grunge Philadelphia's award-winning KOSER is a five-piece alternative rock spectacle fronted by powerhouse vocalist Koser Kara, recognized from NBC's The Voice. Dubbed "galactic glitter grunge," the band delivers gritty guitars, soulful vocals, cinematic storytelling, and electrifying improvisation. Since forming in 2022, KOSER has earned festival wins and regional acclaim, opened NPR's Tiny Desk Contest On The Road Tour, and appeared on WMMR, NBC10, Q102, and more. For fans of art-rock tension, punk urgency, and ferocious dance moves, KOSER delivers the kind of performance that makes you stop walking, stay awhile, and leave a little sweatier than you arrived.

Party/Cover | Pop | Rock Toolshed Jack is a high-energy party band from Pennsylvania, delivering non-stop fun at festivals, casinos, corporate and private events, weddings, and special events. We thrive on crowd energy, feeding off every cheer to power an active and fluid show packed with well-known anthems, party pop, and rock hits from all eras.   Dynamic Performances Our seamless set flows with minimal interruptions, designed to hype the crowd -blending tight musicianship with visual flair for maximum impact at every show.     Our goal is to turn audiences into participants connecting energies together all in pursuit of a great time enjoyed by all.

Clindren's/Family Peggy Gernerd is Miss Maggie Sings! I sing, write, perform and record educational and fun music for children and their families. I started my children's music career by writing material for The Reading Public Library's annual summer reading program and have been writing similar shows since. I sing, play the guitar and ukulele and often incorporate one of a kind puppets (made by a local pupeteer who studied with Jim Henson's daughter). My shows are interactive: I encourage the audience to clap, sing and move to the music. I always incorporate stretching and dancing into my programs. My CD "Stay Healthy With Miss Maggie" was inspired by my two sons when they were little. I have performed at area elementary schools, festivals, public libraries, preschools, churches, museums and other events. This marks the third year I have performed at Musikfest. I am always ready to energize and inspire both children and their families to enjoy learning through music!

Indie | Pop | Rock Inspired by queer icons, Lilith Fair musicians, 60s/70s rock, femme-fronted-indie-pop, and musical theatre: Miss Olivia sings nostalgically with a modern twist. She hopes her music reminds you that you are the narrator of your own story - and that we're here to co-create through community. Using songs of all shapes and sizes, Miss Olivia carefully recounts her own life's lessons and observations in a way that is comforting and unique. She does this all with a talented band alongside her! Her dream is to perform on stages across the world playing her music, connecting with all kinds of fans but most likely a significantly LGBTQ+ fanbase, like her. Poetry, both accompanied by music and not, has always been a huge interest of Olivia's and is what initially led her to wanting to write songs back in 2022. Thank you to Musikfest 2026 for inviting her to play her first ever music festival!

Bluegrass | Folk | Americana | Rock Emerging in 2025 from Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, After August blends folk, bluegrass and singer songwriter into a genre-defying sound.   After August is a sophisticated bluegrass duo from Bethlehem, PA built on the heart and soul of music. From classic covers, to brand new originals and all the zany stuff in between, you're sure to have a beautiful time with these musicians. Andi and Ella have both been solo acoustic performers since 2019. Having met in high school for music and music production, the two quickly bonded over one epic concert and shared music taste, inspiring them to create a sound of their own. The duo is eager to chase the euphoric energy of music and in turn, share it with people who are just as creative and adventurous. Join them in jamming on tunes from the 60's to present day, and explore the depths of their minds with their originals.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Alternative | Classical | Oldies Best friends, Juke & Ben, are an acoustic duo that brings music of all genres to any event that values quality, energy, and good times. The Duo are in the Band Code 201 and decided to further their love for music by bringing tunes from across the decades to a more intimate setting with fun and interactive tunes everyone knows and loves.

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Genre: Blues | Funk | R&B/Soul | Rock Philly's own blues and soul drenched singer and songwriter Deb Callahan Philly's own blues and soul drenched singer and songwriter Deb Callahan has been performing, touring, recording and entertaining music fans both regionally and around the United States since the late 1990s. She has graced the stages of many festivals nationwide from The Monterey Bay Blues Festival in Monterey, CA, The Springing the Blues Fest in Jacksonville, FL, The Bayfront Blues Fest in Duluth, MN, The Briggs Farm Blues Fest in Nescopeck, PA to WXPN's XPonential Music Festival in Philly. She celebrated the release of her 6th studio album "Backbone" in March 2023 for which she received rave reviews, national/international radio play and charted for over 6 months on The Roots Music Report Top 50 Blues albums peaking at #2 in the blues category. Often compared to Bonnie Raitt, Susan Tedeschi and Etta James, Callahan has developed a unique sound all her own.

Pop | Rock Ted Donatelli (formerly of The Aardvarks), Steve Placotaris (Truth & Soul), and Lee LaBar (Hattrick) make up the trio Rock Steady. Covering rock and soft rock from the 70's, 80's, and 90's. America, Little River Band, Boston, Bee Gees... plus a good diet of AM Gold.

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Alternative | Rock The Nashville-based power trio of vocalist Julia Lauren Bullock, guitarist Jake Ohlbaum, and drummer Rob Bodley are a band boldly embracing their wildest and most unpredictable impulses. The Foxies infuse their songs with equal parts raw emotion and a musical sophistication that comes from years of devotion to their craft and their high-energy live show. The arrival of their new music in 2024, all three members feel more driven than ever when it comes to fulfilling their mission as a band.

Alternative | Indie | Rock Cultivated in the eternal city of Brotherly Love, TIOGA creates music that sounds like if Jack Antonoff and Brandon Flowers went to a karaoke bar and sang Talking Heads songs until they got kicked out and then mumbled David Bowie songs to themselves on the walk home. The group marries their meaningful lyricism and endearing style of alternative indie with a hearty bass and danceable synth-pop sound. Their homebase now extends beyond the confines of Philadelphia, and as far as the Big Apple.

artist: Craig Thacher Jazz Experience Featuring Chico Huff, Cliff Starkey, Billy Wear & Nelson Hill
venue: Lutron Jazz Cabaret Stage at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Blues | Funk | Jazz Featuring members of the Craig Thatcher Band (Chico Huff, Cliff Starkey & Billy Wear) along with very special guest for this Musikfest 2026 performance, world-class saxophonist Nelson Hill (Maynard Ferguson, Eric Mintel and many others).    Though Craig is best known as a blues/rock based guitarist, this set developed out of Craig's love of funk/jazz/blues (think Crusaders) and a desire to play a new, completely different Musikfest show from CTB's normal material, in an intimate MF setting (Jazz Cabaret) in the Banko Cinema. The band will perform some funky jazz standards as well as original material written by Cliff and Craig.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Indie | Rock | Acoustic George Hrab is a multi-instrumentalist, singer, songwriter, story teller, science advocate, producer, composer, & vigilant defender of the Theory of Gravity. He's written and produced nine independent albums and a concert DVD; published two books; recorded over 950 episodes of the award-winning Geologic Podcast; emceed countless science conferences; was a TEDx speaker; was the drummer and musical director with The Philadelphia Funk Authority for twenty four years; hosts the concert/lecture series Seven Songs with George Hrab on YouTube, and has even performed for President Clinton. He's traveled to four continents promoting critical thinking, science, and skepticism through story and song. He's known for both his thought-provoking, clever, and often hilarious lyrics; as well as for his genre-bending musical acumen.

Alternative | Rock | Grunge Crusasis is the grunge rock brainchild of multi-instrumentalist and songwriter Ryan Smith. Smith, known for traveling the country on his 1984 Honda Sabre motorcycle, his jazz saxophone background and years of touring in an underground metal band has honed his music to what can be called "mountain grunge." The music features riffs that make you feel like you are simultaneously crossing the country on a motorcycle and also exploring an ever expanding cave. Featuring Smith's fiery and wide ranged vocals, the band is as if Led Zeppelin and Queens of the Stone Age had more grunge, metal and atmospheric moments. Flushed out with the impeccable Eric Curley on bass guitar and international touring drummer Melissa Lucciola, the music speaks for itself and draws in audiences during their hypnotic and energetic performances.

artist: Dancing Dream: A Tribute to ABBA
description: Party/Cover | Pop | Rock DANCING DREAM, the Tribute to ABBA - the greatest pop supergroup from Sweden, electrifies audiences of all ages with the best ABBA hits "Mamma Mia", "Dancing Queen" "Take a Chance" "Fernando" and many more. Founded by two European singers in 2009, featured on the Stephen Colbert Show and in large venues across North America, this New York City based ABBA Tribute Band transports audiences back to the happy, groovy 70's. With beautiful harmonies, elaborate costumes and exciting choreography, every show is to be remembered. Because of the recent popularity of "Mamma Mia" the musical and the movie, Dancing Dream performed many concert venues across the country attracting audiences of all age groups. The band toured US couple of times with performances in theaters, and popular clubs and concert venues. This ABBA Tribute Band has a growing number of fans all over the US and beyond. Don't miss this opportunity to slip on your platforms and sing along to your favorite Abba hits.

Blues | Folk | Americana | Rock Appearing at Musikfest for their sixth year, aGirl & aGuy Band brings you original and cover tunes on an entertaining journey through Americana, rock, and blues genres. Featuring splendid harmony vocals, deft guitar, upright bass and percussion/drums, there's a song here for everyone to enjoy.

artist: Life After Dead: A Tribute to Grateful Dead
venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Rock | Tribute | Jam Band Life After Dead is a tribute to the improvisational, indescribable music of The Grateful Dead. The band is comprised of Rich Jeffreys on lead vocals and guitar, Jon Fadem (from Start Making Sense: Talking Heads Tribute) on lead guitar and vocals, Dave Johnsen on bass and vocals, and Zach Martin on drums. Together, they create a sound which, while reminiscent of their collective musical influences, reflects a desire to create exciting new music which stretches the boundaries of imagination, while inspiring their audiences to dance at the same time.

Bluegrass | Folk | Americana | Rock Emerging in 2025 from Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, After August blends folk, bluegrass and singer songwriter into a genre-defying sound.   After August is a sophisticated bluegrass duo from Bethlehem, PA built on the heart and soul of music. From classic covers, to brand new originals and all the zany stuff in between, you're sure to have a beautiful time with these musicians. Andi and Ella have both been solo acoustic performers since 2019. Having met in high school for music and music production, the two quickly bonded over one epic concert and shared music taste, inspiring them to create a sound of their own. The duo is eager to chase the euphoric energy of music and in turn, share it with people who are just as creative and adventurous. Join them in jamming on tunes from the 60's to present day, and explore the depths of their minds with their originals.
Indie | Rock

Alternative | Rock | World  Igor Yuzov was born in Germany, raised in Ukraine and studied in Russia. He grew up in the former Soviet Union, where folk music was the norm and rock'n'roll was illegal. A rebellious streak, led him to seek out the forbidden music. As soon as it became possible, Igor left Russia for America with his "Folk'n'Roll" band Limpopo, and was personally greeted by Ronald Reagan. In 1993, Limpopo won Ed McMahon's Star Search and their popularity began to blossom. In 1995, Igor dreamed that Elvis Presley came to him and told him to start playing rock'n'roll. Igor and his Russian friends became Red Elvises and gave street performances on Santa Monica's 3rd Street Promenade. As their crowds grew larger, the City of Santa Monica ordered them to discontinue their street performances. Evolving over the years, Igor's music has been labeled "Siberian Surf Rock" which contains humorous lyrics and grooves that forces his audience to dance.

artist: The Warped Band - Ultimate Tribute to Warped Tour
venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Party/Cover | Tribute The Warped Band is the Official Tribute to Warped Tour, resurrecting the spirit of the legendary festival that defined generations of summers with music, community, and nonstop energy. Endorsed by Kevin Lyman, founder of Warped Tour, and by Warped Tour itself, The Warped Band is the only tribute officially recognized to carry on that legacy.   As the Official Tribute, The Warped Band celebrates the full spectrum of genres that made Warped Tour iconic, spanning pop punk, emo, ska, hardcore, metalcore, and skate punk. Every performance captures the chaos, passion, and discovery that made Warped Tour more than just a concert. It was an all‑day experience and a cultural moment.
Alternative | Country

Indie | Pop Tess Becket is an emerging voice in the indie rock scene, known for her emotionally raw music and multifaceted talents as a producer, songwriter, and audio engineer. Her sound, inspired by many women in music, including King Princess, Hayley Williams, and Julien Baker, blends alternative rock and pop with heartfelt lyricism. Since 2023, she has successfully released four singles, amassing over 100,000 Spotify streams, and completed her first tour in the U.S.

artist: School of Rock Easton: The Bank Street Band
description: Alternative | Rock This year School of Rock Easton is presenting 2 acts. The first is our Best of the 90s Summer Camp, which will include current students as well as young musicians joining us for the summer.  The second is our Bank Street Band. The Bank Street Band is a collection of students from the Easton School of Rock location in the Lehigh Valley that represent the school and the program. Our goal is to get our kids in front of as many people as we can in as many different situations as we can. Our kids get to play with other house bands from other School of Rock locations through the area. 2025-2026 Bank Street Band Members CLEMENTINE P COLE P COLE Z ELLIE Z JUDY D JULES W KEIRA D LAILA A LIAM L LUKAS B MICHELLE L ROWAN P

Funk | Party/Cover | R&B | Soul Brass With Class is a high-energy 9-piece powerhouse. Our band delivers an unforgettable live experience spanning across a couple of decades of iconic music. Featuring dynamic male and female lead vocals, a tight rhythm section (guitar, bass, keys, and drums), and a vibrant horn section, we bring rich harmonies, driving grooves, and bold brass to every stage. We celebrate the greatest songs with authenticity and style. Whether it's a packed dance floor or a concert stage, we create a show that's timeless, energetic, and impossible to sit still through. Check out our highlight video: https://youtu.be/FlNhafgPW24?si=jLp0YRqoAtD6v-F4

Street Performer | Juggler | Comedian The Flaming Ginger is a fast paced comedy and juggling show. From ground to sky he brings the laughs and backs them up with skills and thrills. Winner of both national and international performance competitions, he will quickly become a highlight of your day.

Street Performer The Cate Great Show combines high-level circus and original comedy into a sharp, witty, big-hearted show that plays to all audiences. She is an award-winning twenty year veteran of circus, stage, and street. Trained at the Quebec City Circus School she's performed in over 11 different countries including: Australia, New Zealand, Dubai, Italy, and the UK as well as most of the US and Canada.

Indie | Pop | Singer-Songwriter Kenzie Lynn is a 19 year old singer-songwriter currently studying songwriting at Belmont University. Her music stands out for its thoughtful storytelling and compelling melodies. Kenzie's most recent song "Design" is out now and follows up the 10+ singles she has released beginning in 2021. Since moving to Nashville, she has played many writers' rounds at places such as the Comadore, Stillery Midtown, Pooky Jane's, and more. You can follow along on her music journey on all social media platforms at (@) kenzielynnofficial.

Blues | Pop | R&B | Soul ​Pentley Holmes is a musician and creative writer from New Jersey. A self taught guitarist and vocalist, he plays a mix of contemporary blues, rock, and soul. His music is defined by a raw, authentic sound that connects with audiences in both large venues and intimate settings. Currently, he is focused on performing live and developing new written projects that explore his personal journey and the human experience.

Alternative | Party/Cover | Pop Modern Music Makers (M3) is the music industry program of the Abington School District in Abington, PA. The program was founded upon the concepts of performance and production in modern music, as well as exploring artist development and music business applications through marketing and media development

Funk | Party/Cover | Rock THE FUNK ROCKERS are a high-energy eight-piece powerhouse delivering an unforgettable live music experience that keeps dance floors packed and audiences moving. Featuring the dynamic Lehigh Valley Horns, the band brings a bold, soulful sound to exciting dance hits spanning the '60s, '70s, '80s, and today. Their nonstop party atmosphere blends funk, soul, pop, rock, and R&B with exceptional musicianship and crowd-pleasing energy. Their extensive repertoire includes favorites from legendary artists such as Earth, Wind & Fire, Michael Jackson, Chicago, Tower of Power, The Commodores, Bruce Springsteen, Amy Winehouse, Donna Summer, Bruno Mars, Stevie Wonder, and many more. Known for electrifying performances and surprise guest appearances, every FUNK ROCKERS show is a unique celebration guaranteed to keep audiences energized from the first note to the final encore. You will not be disappointed!

Alternative | Rock | Classic Rock Roi and the Secret People are a high-energy original alt-rock band hailing from Pennsylvania's Lehigh Valley, delivering punchy, feel-good rock with an edge. Their sound blends indie grit, classic hooks, and hard-rock drive from both sides of the Atlantic, with a subtle Southern sway that keeps things loose, soulful, and unmistakably theirs.   A regular presence across the tri-state area, the band's live sets pull from a deep catalogue of five full releases and multiple singles, all original and all built to move a room. Known for keeping audiences on their toes, RSP also slip in the occasional unexpected cover-reimagining artists as wide-ranging as David Bowie and Joss Stone-without ever losing their own identity.    The band has shared stages with an impressive range of artists, including The Smithereens, The Quireboys, Enuff Z'Nuff, The Black Moods, Spread Eagle, The Midnight Devils, Pretty Boy Floyd, Drake Bell, and a sold-out New Jersey show alongside actor-musician Corey Feldman.

Alternative | Pop | Rock  The Joe Miralles Trio was formed after Joe Miralles spent years performing and recording as a solo artist. Following his 2015 solo EP *Five*, he expanded into a full band format, teaming up with other musicians to create a richer, collaborative sound.   The core lineup has included:  Joe Miralles- lead vocals, guitar, songwriter Gary Houk - drums, keyboards Jim Caccamo - Bass,  Vocals   Based in the Greater Philadelphia area, the trio performs regularly across Pennsylvania and surrounding regions.  Style & Influences  Their music blends multiple genres, including:  * pop rock * alternative rock * folk and acoustic rock  Their sound has been compared to artists like Elvis Costello. Tom Petty and the Beatles.   Catch them on Main Street at Musicfest

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Alternative | Folk | Americana | Indie Zach Russack - musician based out of NW, NJ.   Portrayed in his music is the purity of innocence and the harshness of truth. For someone whose music is largely characterized by still introspection, Zach Russack doesn't really slow down. His music, which broaches many subjects and is often marked by intricate acoustic guitar and his soft vocal style, chronicles his reflections on the dichotomies in his life: Growing up in a small town and performing in a big city or building a secure foundation to start a family while pursuing professional goals in the music industry, just to name a few. Whatever the challenge, on whichever side of the coin, Russack's music and performances illustrate he has the versatility to handle them all. Following the release of his second full-length record, Russack continues to shape his world through melody, reflection, and an unflinching passion for connection.

Indie | R&B | Soul | Tribute A musical artist based in eastern Pennsylvania, Chasing Daylight, led by Bobby Siegfried, is a unique group that you really need to experience in all of its flavors. With their contagious blend of indie soul, pop, and R&B, you'll be grooving along right from the start. Their set list is always fresh and evolving, featuring music from across the decades and today, specializing in captivating arrangements and hit songs that only they cover. Their original music is full of excitement and instantly memorable. Chasing Daylight's Coldplay tribute show is a signature musical experience, covering hits from across their entire catalog - something very unique to Chasing Daylight's show. No matter what version of Chasing Daylight you experience, their performances are engaging, fun and feel-good, and full of energy and excitement. Spreading love and light through music is what it's all about. Be good to each other.

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Funk | Jazz | Pop | Rock Bekah Elaine is a Philadelphia-based vocalist known for her genre-blending sound rooted in soul, rock, R&B, and alternative influences. A seasoned performer with a commanding stage presence, she delivers emotionally rich, high-energy sets that transform every show into a shared, unforgettable experience. Her ability to connect with audiences continues to establish her as a standout voice in the city's live music scene.   She joined SoundEVR in summer 2025 as lead singer, bringing her dynamic vocals to the band's dance-driven fusion of Funk, R&B, Pop, Jazz, and House, led by multi-instrumentalist Daniel Spearman. Bekah Elaine is also the lead vocalist of the MISPHITZ, led by Marlon B. Lewis, the official house band for the Philadelphia 76ers and Philadelphia Union. She has performed on major stages across the city, including opening for Ja Rule at The Fillmore, and for Monica and Keith Sweat at Musikfest 2024 with The Cosmic Soul Collective.

Classical | Contemporary Telos Consort is a modular ensemble of two saxophones, two strings, and piano. Formed in 2023 with the goal of uniting old and new instruments, this group of creatives is dedicated to the creation of new work for their unique instrumentation. By programming music by some of the most influential voices and voices-to-be of our time, we encourage genre inclusivity and the presence of the non-traditional. Recent performances include shows at the 92nd Street Y, A4 Collective, and Shapeshifter Lab. The Telos Consort is a Chamber Music America Ensemble Forward grant awardee and will be working with coach Ken Thomson (Bang On A Can All-Stars) throughout 2024.  Recent highlights included partnerships with Chamber Music Society of Lincoln Center and the Harmony Program alongside performances in and around New York City. Recent premieres include those from Cole Reyes, Euna Joh, Gabriel Bouche Caro, Nick Batina, Bobby Ge, Ihlara McIndoe, and Sofia Jen Ouyang, our 2025-2026 Artist-in-Residence.

R&B | Soul | Rock The Circus Hearts are an original Rock N Roll band from Philadelphia, Pa. with a sound rooted in the timeless grooves of the 60's and 70's. Blending classic influences with a modern edge, their music channels the raw emotion, storytelling and unapologetic energy that defined the golden age of Rock. Born from a love of Rock N Roll's heyday, The Circus Hearts bring the sounds of yesteryears roaring into the modern era. With gritty guitars, vintage organ tones and vocals that swing from honeyed harmony to full throttle howl, their sound pays homage to legends like The Rolling Stones, Faces, and Sam Cooke-while carving out a groove all their own. Whether it's a club or a festival stage, The Circus Hearts deliver a live experience that's equal parts energy and soul. Proof that Rock N Roll isn't a memory; it's alive, it's loud and it's still burning.

venue: Lutron Jazz Cabaret Stage at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Jazz Mike Lorenz, guitarist/composer/educator, hails from Bethlehem, PA. For over a decade, Mike has kept a busy schedule as a freelancing performer and educator, leading his trio/quartet in performances up and down the Northeast Corridor on many festivals and club stages. Hailed as "an artist with a relentless and finely focused vision," Lorenz has released many recordings, including his most recent trio record, I'm Not There, released in June 2026. Most recently, he has been heard on releases by Chicago-based saxophonist Michael Hudson-Casanova's group, HeyMan, and releases by Philadelphia bassist Nicholas Krolak.    As a composer, Mike has had several works for string quartet premiered by the Allentown Symphony Organization. In 2024, he completed an artist-in-residency program at the East40, a 40-acre nature learning center in Bethlehem, PA, where he composed a new work for solo guitar, informed and inspired by his time on the grounds.

venue: Wind Creek Steel Stage at PNC Plaza
description: with special guest Kassi Ashton Singer/songwriter and multi-instrumentalist Parker McCollum has reached the ranks of a bona fide Country music superstar by connecting with fans and critics alike through his relatable and authentic sound. McCollum has been named an ‘Artist to Watch' by Rolling Stone, Billboard, SiriusXM, CMT, RIAA, and more with American Songwriter noting, “The Texas native teeters on the edge of next level superstardom.” Music Row listed McCollum as their 2021 Breakout Artist of the Year and Apple also included him as one of their all-genre “Up Next Artists” Class of 2021. A dedicated road warrior, McCollum made his debut at the famed Grand Ole Opry in 2021 and has sold out major concert venues around the U.S. In March 2022, McCollum made his debut at RODEOHOUSTON to a sold-out crowd with over 73,000 tickets sold. In 2025, McCollum played his third consecutive sold out show at Houston's signature event. In 2022, McCollum earned his first ACM Award for New Male Artist of the Year, took home “Breakthrough Video of the Year” (a fully fan-voted honor) at the 2022 CMT Music Awards and scored two back-to-back CMA Awards nominations (2022 & 2023) for New Artist of the Year. He was nominated for a CMA Award for “Song of the Year”(2024) for his explosive Platinum hit “Burn It Down” – marking his third straight nomination. McCollum scored his second ACM Award for “Visual Media of the Year” for his music video for “Burn It Down.” The hit single marks his fourth consecutive #1 – following his other chart toppers “Pretty Heart,” “To Be Loved By You,” and “Handle On You.” McCollum has just released his self-titled fifth studio album, PARKER MCCOLLUM, which is an artistic achievement and includes his current radio single, “What Kinda Man.” PARKER MCCOLLUM shows this legacy making Country superstar at his best and is available now. Special Guest: Kassi Ashton With a little bit of rock, a hell of a lot of soul, MCA Nashville's Kassi Ashton is set to take the music world by storm. “Kassi Ashton is in line for Nashville domination, and she'll do it her way – unapologetically,” proclaims PEOPLE Magazine. The California, Missouri singer/songwriter touched down with a string of critically acclaimed tracks released by UMG Nashville in conjunction with Interscope Records. Her introductory release “California, Missouri” stirred widespread critical acclaim from Taste of Country, CMT, The Boot, and many more. Kassi was also named as an Artist to Watch by Huffington Post, The Tennessean, and radio personality Bobby Bones. Rolling Stone lauds her as “a devilish songwriter who isn't afraid to flip convention on its head,” while Refinery29 claims, “she blends pop aesthetics with a presence doused in no-fucks-left-to-give authenticity,” PAPER Magazine calls her “the next big country superstar,” and American Songwriter notes, “Ashton brings powerful storytelling and a sense of poetry in every release.” In 2025, Kassi received her second nomination for New Female Artist of the Year at the ACMs. Kassi defies convention and continues to deliver her signature style with confessional, catchy storytelling and dynamic vocal performances on songs including critically acclaimed releases “Called Crazy,” “The Straw,” “Drive You Out of My Mind,” “Dates In Pickup Trucks,” “Heavyweight,” “California, Missouri,” “Hopeless,” “Violins,” and more. In September 2024, Kassi released her long-awaited debut album, Made From The Dirt, to praise from People, Rolling Stone, Billboard, and more. The album dishes hard-won lessons rooted in tangled family ties, relationship tussles, and wrenching grief, wrapped up in hook-heavy, country-tinged singalongs, where Kassi's husky, soulful tones will stop you dead in your tracks. On April 25, 2025, Kassi expanded on the album with Made From The Dirt: The Blooms, complete with 5 brand new tracks. Partners  Media Partner

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Folk | Americana | Indie | Pop Nera is a 21 year old, folk/pop singer-songwriter based out of Lancaster PA. She has been performing in the area for the past two years, and has been releasing music since January of 2024 with her debut single and EP "Freeland". She also released another single in July 2024 called "Furhman Park", and her most recent single "cool." Released earlier this year. Her writing style focuses on friendship, relationships, and heartbreak, allowing listeners to relate to her music.

Children's/Family | Folk | Americana Lolly Hopwood is a kindie artist that can perform as a solo act, a duo, trio, or full band. She is based in Doylestown, PA. Her interactive children's music performances are filled with imagination and movement that will really get the audience involved in the show. Using props like parachutes, pom poms, scarves, bubbles, and puppets, the audience won't just be watching the concert, they will be a part of the show.   Lolly's musical influences are classic Sesame Street, Mister Rogers, Harry Nilsson, Cat Stevens, Elizabeth Mitchell, Dan Zanes, Dolly Parton & an array of modern indie artists. She has been compared to Elizabeth Mitchell & the Laurie Berkner Band.

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Folk | Americana Established in Virginia's Shenandoah Valley, Palmyra captures the collective spirit of three Virginia natives: Teddy Chipouras, Mānoa Bell, and Sasha Landon.  Palmyra straddles at least two musical worlds. They are, on one hand, a band from the South that plays traditional instruments and indeed once lived in the old-time locus of Floyd, Virginia. Comparisons to and a kinship with The Avett Brothers and even Old Crow Medicine Show are inevitable. On the other hand, Palmyra writes about grief, gender dysphoria and identity, and coming of age in songs that flirt with soul, post-rock, and even emo; the South, too, is the place of My Morning Jacket, Band of Horses, Cat Power, and now, Palmyra.  The band's first full length LP, Restless, is out now on Oh Boy Records. Framed by moments of struggle, solidarity, and hard-won growth, Restless is an unqualified ringer for anyone who loves the space where the roar of indie rock collides with raw folk.

artist: The Britles: A Tribute to The Beatles
description: Tribute An acoustic Beatles band that brings the timeless classics of the iconic British band to life with an authentic and captivating energy. Their repertoire includes a wide variety of classic Beatles songs from different eras, as well as deep cuts and many tracks that were never performed live by the Beatles themselves.

Horn-powered Classic Rock The Mighty Spectrum Band is returning to Musikfest for it's 4th year! The driving NJ-based band powered by a fun-loving horn section guarantees a night you'll remember. Performing classic hits by Bruce Springsteen, Tom Petty, The Allman Brothers, Joe Cocker, Van Morrison, The Doors and countless others. It's "time to get MIGHTY!"

Children's/Family | Street Performer | Percussion Orbital is a percussion-centric music entertainment group located in Bethlehem, PA. The group features various drumline style routines, choreographed percussion shows and even bagpipes. The group believes in empowering musicians and creating jaw-dropping live musical performances.      Orbital Drumline was founded in April of 2018 by a group of five friends. With a set of used drums we found on Facebook Marketplace and a dream, we set out to create an entertainment percussive experience like no other. Hundreds of shows later - we are still going strong!      The group is made up entirely of Liberty High School alumni and former Grenadier Band Members and almost all of their head drummers from 2017-2025. We are proud of our Bethlehem roots and have received training and mentorship from world renowned musicians and percussionists like Stu Warmington, Tyler Fry, and members of the Red Hot Chili Pipers.

artist: Cisco PL and The Soul Benders
description: Blues | Rock Hailing from Ithaca, NY, Cisco PL and The Soul Benders is a powerhouse original rock ensemble that ignites stages with their soul filled vocals and crowd engaging performances. With band roots ranging from Brooklyn to Rochester, this talented group brings a refreshing blend of roots rock, blues, southern rock energy to every note they play. Cisco PL and The Soul Benders was born out of a shared passion for authentic music and a desire to create something truly exceptional. Their music resonates with the heartfelt emotions while embracing the raw power of rock 'n' roll, resulting in a sound that is as timeless as it is contemporary.

Alternate | Pop mrnoname is the end result of years of musical introspection from songwriter Noah Scheffey and drummer Logan Moyer. Recently releasing their debut album, 'Hallu', a mysterious island that Noah defects to, he realizes the only way that he will be able to escape is if he digs through his mind and truly discovers who he is. It is a self made trial that converses with themes such as existentialism, the yearning to be something greater, the problems in the world and more.

Jazz | Street Performer | New Orleans Friday and Saturday nights, the Big Easy Easton Brass turns Main St. into Bourbon St. as they bring Mardi Gras to Musikfest!!! Open to all ages and skill levels, this community band is bringing a New Orleans tradition to the streets of Easton. A Second Line parade is not one you just watch go by, it's one you participate in. Walk along, Dance along, Sing along, Shake a Tambourine, Fill the Streets with Bubbles or even learn to play an instrument and jump on in, this band wants to blur the line between spectator and participant. Free to join, rehearsals are at the Easton Area Community Center at 901 Washington St.

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Country | Folk | Americana | Indie Arden Webster is a genre-blending musician whose sound fuses introspective lyricism with rich sonic textures, drawing inspiration from country, ambient folk, and alt-pop. With a voice that's both haunting and intimate, Arden crafts songs that explore themes of vulnerability, resilience, and the search for meaning in a chaotic world. Their artistry balances raw emotion with polished production, creating immersive experiences that resonate deeply with listeners. Hailing from Pittsburgh, PA., Arden has been steadily building a loyal following through a string of captivating live performances and heartfelt studio recordings. The debut EP, Flight Risk, was released in July 2025, receiving critical acclaim for its lyrical depth and lush instrumentation.

Jazz | Oldies | Tribute The SwingTime Dolls is an all female vocal group consisting of three of the area's most accomplished vocalists that shine a new light on the tunes of the American Hit Parade of the 1940s and the 1960s classics of the Vietnam era. Drawing on inspiration from The Andrews Sisters, the Dolls pride themselves on tight and intricate harmonies that are sure to keep any listener engaged. Because all three women are well versed in musical theatre, The SwingTime Dolls brings another facet of entertainment to their audiences with pin-up styled costumes and energetic choreography. A SwingTime Dolls show is sure to capture the hearts of any age with the original covers of timeless classics such as Glenn Miller's "In The Mood" and even some modern hits from George Michael, The Bangles, and The Spice Girls. The Dolls will take you on a sentimental journey back in time with a show you're sure to remember.

Latin | Pop | Rock Fabricio Orihuela, known artistically as Fabry, is a Uruguayan artist based in the United States since 2010. A musician, singer, and guitarist, his style blends romantic pop, ballads, and Latin rock, bringing warmth, emotion, and authenticity to every performance.   Fabry is a well-loved artist in the local music scene, performing regularly at bars, restaurants, and private events throughout the area. His versatile repertoire and heartfelt interpretation allow him to connect naturally with diverse audiences.   In addition to his live music career, Fabry is also a music producer and the owner of Ático Sound Productions, a studio where he currently works with emerging artists, helping them develop their sound, record their music, and create professional content to grow their careers.

venue: The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America Zinzenplatz
description: Indie | Pop | Rock LA based indie-pop trio Koyal is made up of best friends Pooja Prabakaran (vocals/bass), Noah Weinstein (guitar/producer) and Wallace (drums) who met while earning engineering degrees in college. Unfortunately for them, they preferred writing music over making lots of money in tech.   The group's namesake is a koyal, a black Southeast Asian songbird that Pooja heard every day outside her window during the 3 years she lived in India. The three friends began making songs with an electric blend of indie rock, synthy pulsing pop, and relatable lyricism. Koyal has played alongside the Barenaked Ladies, Moon Taxi, & Crash Test Dummies and has written music with members of GROUPLOVE.    In 2025, Koyal released their sophomore album "breathe in. breathe out." Upon its release, the album received high praise from Pleaser Magazine, The LUNA Collective, The Knockturnal and more. The Honey POP wrote that the LP is, "incredible... dynamic... the perfect mix of light indie and edgier rock."

venue: Air Products Americaplatz at Levitt Pavilion SteelStacks
description: Pop | Rock Ryan Cabrera is the singer and songwriter behind ubiquitous smashes such as "On the Way Down," "True" and "Shine On" as well as albums including 2004's Take It All Away, which hit #8 on the Billboard Top 200. Ryan was also featured heavily on MTV's "Ashlee Simpson Show" and "The Hills".

artist: Hello, I Must Be Going: A Tribute to Phil Collins
description: Pop | Rock | Tribute Hello I Must Be Going, the ultimate Phil Collins Tribute brings to the stage the music of the Genesis, Live Wembley 1987 Show. This iconic concert brings back a decade of some of the best Genesis era music from the late '70s to the late '80s. The band recreates some of the great classic hits from Duke to Invisible Touch recordings. Some of the songs include - Home by the Sea, Mama, Tonight Tonight Tonight, Follow You Follow Me, and many many more. Including some old classics. The band captures the True Essence of Genesis in their Prime. Come and experience this Iconic show and you will feel like you are back in time seeing it for the first time.

Oldies | Party/Cover | Rock THE TRUANTS are thrilled to be making their 11th appearance at Musikfest, the absolute highlight of every gigging summer for us. For those of you who have yet to experience THE TRUANTS, we are a five-man force of rock nature out of New York. We play British Invasion and American covers, and as is our mantra: NOTHING AFTER 1967! We play the songs you love and the songs you forgot love....as you remember them.  Musikfest began featuring us on "British Invasion Day" in 2015 at the Festplatz and though we have now moved to the PNC Stadtplatz, our wonderful supporters from Bethlehem and beyond have followed us. Beatles, Stones, DC5, Zombies, Hollies, Kinks, Van Morrison, Yardbirds, Gerry and the Pacemakers, Searchers, Animals...oh my! 90 minutes of relentlessly sing-along, thoroughly danceable hits, B-sides and deep cuts. Can't wait!

venue: Lyrikplatz at Frank Banko Alehouse Cinemas
description: Pop | Singer-Songwriter Rue Vega isn't the cool girl. A multi-instrumentalist singer songwriter who defies definition, she is making space for the outsiders- for the ones who spend the weekends alone instead of at parties, and whose crushes always seem to be unrequited and impossible. Her music pushes for rejecting the beauty standard; for reclaiming trauma and making it your strength. Blending the unashamed boldness of Billie Eilish, the messy honesty of Gracie Abrams, and the cinematic sparkle of Chappell Roan, Rue Vega is bulldozing her way into the pop scene.
